boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: The Boston Children's Museum pass offers half price admission for up to four (4) people.

concord-museum.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
concord-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
concord-museum.benefits_text: This pass admits up to 4 at $5-off each. Pickup the pass the day before or the day of the reservation date. This pass MUST be returned to Acton Memorial Library before the library opens the day after use. It can be placed in the outside book drop if the library is closed. Overdue passes are subject to a fine of $10 per day.

davis-farmland.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
davis-farmland.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
davis-farmland.benefits_text: This pass admits up to six (6) people to either Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze at the gate with a savings of $10 OFF per ticket. This is a coupon pass and does not need to be returned.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: This pass admits up to three (3) people at half price. Pickup the pass up to six (6) days in advance of the reservation date. This pass MUST be returned to the library it was checked out from (either Acton Memorial Library or West Acton Citizens' Library) before the library opens the day after use. It can be placed in the outside book drop if the library is closed. Overdue passes are subject to a fine of $10 per day. This pass is provided by the Friends of the Acton Libraries.

ecotarium.description: The EcoTarium is a unique indoor-outdoor museum in Worcester, MA. Set in an urban oasis, the museum offers a chance to walk through the treetops, take a multimedia journey through the galaxy at a digital planetarium, meet wildlife, stroll nature trails, and get hands-on with family-friendly exhibits.
ecotarium.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
ecotarium.benefits_text: This pass offers a 50% discount admission for up to two (2) adults and two (2) children in one vehicle. Children under two are admitted for free.

garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.benefits_text: This pass admits up to two (2) adults at 50% discounted admission, and up to two (2) children at $1 admission. Pickup the pass up to six (6) days in advance of the reservation date. This pass MUST be returned to Acton Memorial Library before the library opens the day after use. It can be placed in the outside book drop if the library is closed. Overdue passes are subject to a fine of $10 per day.

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: This pass allows four (4) people at the Harvard Museum of Natural History or the Peabody admission desks at 50% off regular museum admission rates . Includes access to all four (4) HMSC museums.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: Admits up to two (2) visitors at a discounted price of $10 per admission ticket. (discounted from the regular fee of $20 per ticket).

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: This pass allows admission for up to four (4) people at $5 per person. Children under the age of 18 are always free.

john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.benefits_text: Passes are no longer required—the presentation of a valid library card grants the cardholder 50% off of one admission ticket—but please place a reservation for the desired date of your visit.”

mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.description: Mass Audubon maintains about 100 wildlife sanctuaries in Massachusetts, more than half of which may be visited for walks on trails and visits to nature centers and museums. Among these is Drumlin Farm in Lincoln, a working farm with animals and crops, plus exhibits that include native wildlife, such as owls, foxes and deer. A shop on the premises sells items related to nature, such as bird feeders and binoculars.
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.benefits_text: This pass admits 4 people to Drumlin Farm and other Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuaries for $2 per visitor over the age of 2. This pass is a coupon and does not need to be returned to Acton Memorial Library. This pass is provided by the Friends of the Acton Libraries.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: This pass offers free general parking at many Massachusetts State Parks. Pickup the pass the day before or the day of the reservation date. This pass is not valid for camping or parking at the following locations – Quabbin Reservoir Boat Launch Areas, Squantum Point, North Point, any metered facilities, or Draw Seven Commuter lots. This pass MUST be returned to Acton Memorial Library before the library opens the day after use. It can be placed in the outside book drop if the library is closed. Overdue passes are subject to a fine of $10 per day.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: This pass allows admission for two (2) adults at $15 each and up to 4 children ages 7-17 at $10 each. Children 6 and under are free. The pass is good ONLY for the date listed. It cannot be used on another date.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: • This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. • Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. • Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. • When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or if you would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). • If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Library pass holders no longer need to make advance reservation s. Tickets cannot be pre-purchased online nor over the phone. The original, dated pass must be presented at the Aquarium Box Office (“General Admission” line) at the time of the visit. Library pass holders will be granted immediate entry after purchasing discounted tickets, even if the Aquarium is sold out to the general public at that time. Passes can be scanned directly off the phone or other device OR may be printed. Barcode must be included. Patrons who do not have an original pass will not receive the discount. Each coupon admits up to 4 people for a 50% off reduction from the regular admission rate, per person . Coupon only applies to adult and child rates. Reduced-rate admission payment must be paid at the Aquarium Box Office to gain entry. Children under the age of 3 are not included in the admission count and are always admitted free. Please visit neaq.org/visit/offers-and-discounts for details. Review the Aquarium’s website at neaq.org/visit/accessibility ahead of visiting to ensure a safe and accessible experience. Library coupons may not be used in conjunction with school or group visits, nor be applied toward Aquarium programs, Simons Theatre tickets, Whale Watch tickets, or combination tickets. Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. Hours of operation are subject to change without notice. Before leaving home, we encourage you to visit our website, neaq.org , for details about hours, exhibits, schedules, prices, directions, parking, and safety guidelines. If you have questions, you may call the New England Aquarium Membership Department at 617-973-6555, weekdays, 9:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m., or email members@neaq.org .

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: This pass offers a 50% discount on general admission for up to two (2) people. Pickup the pass the day before or the day of the reservation date.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: The Peabody Essex Museum pass offers discounted tickets of $12 each for up to two (2) people.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: The DeCordova and Fruitlands Museums are included in the list of Trustees of the Reservations Properties. For a full list of all Trustees of the Reservations properties, please go to thetrustees.org . The GO Pass admits two adults and children under 18 at the family membership admission price (at Trustees properties that charge an admission fee). GO Passes are valid for admission only. Not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, inns, campgrounds, special events and programs, equipment rentals, or in place of any permits or passes. This pass is provided by the Friends of the Acton Libraries.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Provides discounted admission for a group of up to 6 individuals per pass to the Franklin Park and Stone Zoos. Admission costs with the pass are $9 per adult and $6 per child. Under 2 is free. Pass can only be used for daytime admission. They can't be used for Stone Zoo Zoolights or Franklin Park Zoo Boston Lights.

the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.museum_name: The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.description: The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models.
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.benefits_text: Print from home pass Pass allow 4 people free.

bedrock-gardens.description: Originally an historic farm and private garden, Bedrock Gardens is now a public garden that features unusual botanical specimens and sculpture by co-founder Jill Nooney. The 37-acre site is home to over a thousand different plant species, an 18th century farmhouse, various gardens, a wildlife pond, two miles of woodland paths, a Japanese tea house, and many structural elements that contribute to the uniqueness of the landscape. They are typically closed during the winter months, so check the website for details on the schedule.
bedrock-gardens.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
bedrock-gardens.benefits_text: Print from home pass. Pass is for free admission for a family to Bedrock Gardens (not special events). -Valid on date of admission only. -To be turned in on admission. -More information can be found at www.bedrockgardens.org.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Pass may be printed from home or presented on a smart phone. Pass admits up to 4 people at 1/2 price admission. Pre-purchase your discounted tickets online. Coupon only good for date listed. Please check the museum website for current hours.

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: Print from home pass. 50 % discount for up to 4 tickets. For Tickets: www.bostonharborislands.org/ferry-tickets/ Select a date to visit Spectacle Island or Georges Island Select up to 4 tickets then continue to Booking Details Enter the library's unique code found printed on the pass in the "Coupon/Voucher Code" field and click apply Complete your transaction & bring your phone or printed copy of your barcode to board the ferry Summer Schedule: June 20 – September 2, 2024 Boston to Spectacle Island: Monday through Friday Boston to Georges Island: Monday through Friday Hingham to Peddocks Island: Monday through Friday Fall Schedule: September 5 – September 22 Boston to Georges Island: Thursday through Sunday End of Season Schedule: September 27 – October 11 Boston to Georges Island: Friday through Sunday

childrens-museum-of-new-hampshire.description: The Children's Museum of New Hampshire offers opportunities for children and families to explore art, science, and culture through dozens of activities and exhibits. Among these are the Thinkering and Steam Innovation Labs; Dino Detective; Music Matrix & Sound Watch; Naturalist's Study; Cave Explorers; and Post Office. The recently re-opened Dover Adventure Playground features a life-size flat-bottomed vintage gundalow boat; a net climber; swings and slides; a 3-story Garrison tower; a 1,200 pound, 3x8 foot climbable Alewife granite fish sculpture; a splash pad; musical instruments; and a life-sized “whale's tail” sculpture perched on the roof of the Dover Pool. Regularly scheduled special events take place throughout the year, including the Dover Mini Maker Faire; Teddy Bear Clinic + Picnic; Toddlerfest; 5K Road Race + Kid-Venture Course; and Earth Day Celebration; among others. A museum shop is on the premises.
childrens-museum-of-new-hampshire.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
childrens-museum-of-new-hampshire.benefits_text: Pick up pass at the library. 50% off admission for up to 4. Before visiting all guests must make a reservation and pre-pay using code listed on the pass via the museum website. Pass must accompany prepaid tickets. Can only be used on date printed on pass.

concord-museum.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
concord-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
concord-museum.benefits_text: Pick up and return pass to library Admits up to 4 at $5 off each admission

The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: Print from home pass. Pass allows 3 people admission at 1/2 price.

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: Pick up coupon pass at library. Admits 4 people at 50%. Admission good for Harvard Museum of Natural History and Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology (hours daily 9 AM-5 PM), The Harvard Museum of Ancient Near East and Collection of Historic Scientific Instruments ( hours Sunday -Friday 11AM- 4PM)

historic-new-england.address: 141 Cambridge Street, Administrative Offices: Otis House, Boston, MA 02114
historic-new-england.description: Historic New England is a historic preservation organization which owns and operates historic house museums and properties open to the public throughout most of New England. The majority of the 36 properties are in Massachusetts, but locations exist in all NE states but Vermont. These include Castle Tucker (Wiscasset, ME), the Jackson House (Portsmouth, NH), the Spencer-Pierce-Little Farm, which (Newbury, MA), Roseland Cottage (Woodstock, CT), the Otis House (Boston, MA), the Lyman Estate (Waltham, MA), Gropius House (Lincoln, MA), and the Arnold House (Lincoln, RI). Styles represented include Federal, Colonial Revival, Victorian, Queen Anne, Arts & Crafts, and Modern, among many others. The website provides much detail, including directions, contact information, and history of each property.
historic-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
historic-new-england.benefits_text: Pick up coupon at library. Pass will admit 2 adults and children under 18 years old. Go to HistoricNewEngland.org for list of properties. Each property has their own days and hours they are open. Reservations can be made at several locations for the day you have the pass reserved.

the-house-of-seven-gables.description: The House of the Seven Gables, one of the oldest surviving timber-framed mansion houses in continental North America, was the setting of Nathaniel Hawthorne's famous 1851 novel of the same name. The historic site which includes the house (a National Historic Landmark District) includes a guided tour of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion (the House of the Seven Gables) and visits to the Nathaniel Hawthorne Birthplace, the Colonial Revival seaside gardens, and the Counting House, with a maritime discovery zone called “Kids' Cove at The Gables.” (An audio tour of the gardens, grounds, and waterfront is available through the UniGuide App.) Lectures and seasonal living history labs are scheduled throughout the year, and a changing annual exhibition examines various aspects of the institution's 350 years of history. There is a gift shop on the premises. The website has virtual field trips and interactive exhibitions that can provide helpful background information before a visit.
the-house-of-seven-gables.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-house-of-seven-gables.benefits_text: Pick up coupon pass at the library. Patrons receive 50% off admission for up to 4 people. Patrons must purchase tickets online in advance at 7gables.org using LIBRARYPATRON promo code. Pass must accompany tickets.

The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: Print from home pass. Pass allows 2 people at $10 per person (if you book more, the museum will charge you the balance upon entry). Reserve your timed-tickets in advance on the ICA website at www.icaboston.org and select the "Library Pass Discount" price or call ICA Visitor Services at 617-478-3100. Present your printed passes at the Visitor Services desk to purchase tickets onsite. Visitors must present their e-ticket along with their paper library pass when checking in at the museum. Please note that purchases made on the ICA website or over the phone incur a $4 service fee per ticket.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Pick up pass from Library Pass will allow up to 4 people admission at $5 per person. It is recommended to purchase tickets ahead of time. Pass alone does not guarantee admission.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Pick up and return pass to library. Pass may be picked up the day before use. Pass must be returned no later than the day after use. Pass allows 1 car and passengers into the state park or beach. Pass is for day use only and CANNOT be used for camping. Only Andover residents may reserve and use this pass.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Print from home pass. A library pass will not be accepted for admission to the Museum. Bring the code or printed pass to use onsite to reserve same-day tickets. Each pass admits 2 adults for $10 each (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7 – 17 for $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for general admission for children ages 7-17 (cost is $10/ticket). Children age 6 and under are free.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: Print from home pass. This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). Code of Conduct for MOS- https://www.mos.org/visit/code-of-conduct

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Print from home pass. This coupon allows 50% off admission for 4 people to the New England Aquarium. Only applies to adult and child pricing. This is not a ticket. Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. Valid only for Aquarium admission. This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. Void if altered or copied. No cash value. Hours of operation are subject to change.

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: Pick up and return pass to the library. Pass admits 2 people at 1/2 price. Online pre-reservations are no longer required for general admission visits. Online pre-registration tickets are still available for those that would like to purchase tickets in advance at www.towerhillbg.org/planyourvisit using access code LIBDI Pick up the library pass the day before your visit. You will need to show it AND your booked tickets upon arrival at the Garden.

new-england-quilt-museum.description: The New England Quilt Museum, part of the Lowell National Historic Park, is fittingly located not far from the old textile manufacturing plants that made the city famous during the industrial revolution. Since its opening in 1987, it has been committed to both craft and fine arts quilts, with a permanent collection, special exhibitions, a library and resource center, classrooms, and a gift shop.
new-england-quilt-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-quilt-museum.benefits_text: Print from home pass. Pass admits 2 people for free.

new-hampshire-philharmonic-orchestra.address: 44 Geremonty Drive, Seifert Performing Arts Center, Salem, NH 03079
new-hampshire-philharmonic-orchestra.description: The New Hampshire Philharmonic Orchestra has presented classical music concerts with their mix of student, amateur, and professional musicians for over a century in various venues. In 2018, they took up residence in the Seifert Performing Arts Center in Salem, built the previous year. Among the themed concerts that allow them to present well-known pieces of classical repertory, they typically schedule Holiday Pops concerts in December.
new-hampshire-philharmonic-orchestra.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-hampshire-philharmonic-orchestra.benefits_text: Print from Home pass Pass allows 20% discount for online tickets

north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Print from home pass Pass admits up to 5 at 50% off Printed or digital pass MUST be presented at the museum to receive the discount even if purchased online.

plimoth-patuxet-museums.description: Plimoth Patuxet Museums offers a living history that tells the story of the English colonists who became known as “Pilgrims” and the Native Americans with whom they interacted. A big draw is the re-creation of the original settlement, populated with interpreters in period dress with whom one may interact that are based on actual historical figures. Native People from various nations in historically accurate dress provide information about the Wampanoag—the first tribe the Pilgrims encountered—at a home site based on how the group would have lived in the 17th century. In addition to the two settlements, the museum includes a visitor center, the Craft Center, barns that contain rare and heritage animal breeds, and a grist mill that manufactures cornmeal. Plymouth Patuxet Museums is also responsible for the replica of the Mayflower, the ship that brought the Pilgrims to the New World.
plimoth-patuxet-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
plimoth-patuxet-museums.benefits_text: Print from home This coupon must be presented to Plimoth Patuxet Museums guest services staff upon arrival at the Museum for Memorial Hall Library patrons to purchase a Heritage Pass, Combination Pass, or Plimoth Patuxet Only pass for up to six individuals at the reduced rate of $16 per adult; $10 per child ages 5-17. This coupon is for one time use only by the patron.

salem-witch-museum.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
salem-witch-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
salem-witch-museum.benefits_text: Pick up and return pass to library. Pass admits up to 6 people discounted admission. Advanced ticket purchase is required. Please visit the website www.salemwitchmuseum.com to purchase your tickets and enter the code from the pass into the coupon code box to receive discount. Pass MUST be presented at time of admission or full price will be charged. Open Daily 10 am to 5 pm July and August open daily 10 am to 7 pm Pass is NOT valid in October Please present this pass upon arrival to receive your tickets. If you cannot provide pass, you will be charged full admission fee. Admission with pass: Adult/Senior: $14 (regular admission: $19/$17.50) Child: $11.75 (regular admission: $16)

seacoast-science-center.address: 570 Ocean Boulevard, Odiorne Point State Park, Rye, NH 03870
seacoast-science-center.description: Located within Odiorne Point State Park in Rye, NH, the Seacoast Science Center has been providing educational experiences on behalf of New Hampshire State Parks since 1992. The center includes hands-on science exhibits (undersea exploration to marine debris), live animal exhibits which feature creatures that live in the rapidly changing Gulf of Maine ecosystem; and many special events, like “Music-by-the-Sea” concerts and “Sippin' for Seals” (dancing and drinks to support the Marine Mammal rescue Program).
seacoast-science-center.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
seacoast-science-center.benefits_text: Print from home. Pass admits 2 adults and up to 4 children/grandchildren into the Seacoast Science Center at 50% off the cost of regular admission. Pass holder receives 10% discount in the Nature Store Library pass does NOT include the State Park admission. Park fees are $4 per adult (ages 12+) and $2 per child (ages 6-11) . Office use only: _____#adults _____#children _____# under3 _____ # seniors

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: Pick up coupon pass at the library Pass admits 2 adults (children under 18 are free) Check the location you plan to visit for hours of operation

wenham-museum.description: Wenham Museum is a history museum celebrating the treasures of childhood and New England family life across four centuries, with an emphasis on the North Shore. The museum's collections feature a world-renowned doll collection and dollhouses, costumes and textiles, model trains, antique toys, historic photographs, and the interactive Family Discovery Gallery. Admission includes a tour of the historic Claflin-Gerrish-Richards House, built at the end of the 17th century. In addition, there are rotating special programs and exhibitions. A gift shop is on the premises.
wenham-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
wenham-museum.benefits_text: Print from home pass Museum passes are good for half off general admission for up to four visitors.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pick up coupon at library. Pass allows up to 6 people discounted admission ($9.00/adult and $6.00/child) to the Stone Zoo or the Franklin Park Zoo Library coupon holders are no longer required to make a timed ticket reservation. Patrons may walk in with the library coupon and purchase tickets for the discounted rate.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: This pass admits up to four (4) visitors at half-price admission. You will receive an email coupon from the Library. You then MUST visit https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing/ to purchase your tickets at half price. On the day of your visit, you will need to present your museum tickets AND the library coupon to gain entry.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: Advance reservations are no longer required or accepted. This pass admits up to three (3) visitors at half-price admission. You will receive a coupon that you can present to staff on the day of your visit.

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: This pass admits up to four (4) visitors at half-price admission. This pass is a physical coupon pass that you can pick up at the Robbins Library, but not the Fox branch. You will need to select a reservation date, but you can apply the coupon discount on any date that you decide to go. Admission includes entry to: The Harvard Museum of Natural History The Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology The Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East The Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: This pass admits up to two (2) people at $10 each. Library Passes are not valid for performances, events, or films You will receive an email from the Library that will act as your coupon. You can purchase your tickets online at https://my.icaboston.org/events , or over the phone at 617-478-3100, or in person on the day of your visit.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.

In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.

You will receive a promo code, which you can redeem online when you are buying your tickets. You can also redeem the promo code in person, but the Museum strongly recommends buying your tickets in advance.

To reserve tickets ahead of time, please contact the Museum at 617-723-2500

They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.

new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: This pass admits up to four (4) visitors at half-price admission. You will receive a promo code that can be redeemed on the date of your visit. This code cannot be redeemed online.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: This pass admits up to two (2) adults at $12 each. You will receive a promo code that you can apply when purchasing tickets online. Advance reservations are strongly encouraged.

boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 4 people at 1/2 off admission price. Click "Reserve a Pass" below to select the date you would like to visit and receive a discount coupon to the Children's Museum. Your email confirmation will include the printable discount coupon and instructions for ordering timed tickets to the Children's Museum with the library discount. Timed tickets are currently required to enter the museum and your discount coupon and entry dates must match. Pass cannot be combined with other discounts.

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: The pass provides 50% off up to 4 ferry tickets from Boston to Spectacle Island and Georges Island or from Hingham to Peddocks Island. This is a SEASONAL pass available mid-May through early October. Spring Schedule *May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays - Georges Island Summer Schedule *June 16 - September 1: Daily - Georges Island *June 15 - September 7: Daily (Except Tuesdays) - Peddocks Island from Hingham *July 15 - September 1: Daily Spectacle Island Fall Schedule Spectacle Island *September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays *September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays Peddocks Island *September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays The most up-to-date information can be found at: Ferry Ticket Information

concord-museum.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
concord-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
concord-museum.benefits_text: This pass is a laminated pass that is checked out and must be returned. Once reserved, you may pick up the pass either the day of use or the day before if the library is open. Check with the museum for any additional information about your visit including safety guidelines and timed ticketing requirements. Each pass admits up to 4 people at $5 off per person from the regular admission price. Cannot be combined with other discounts. Not valid for member pricing.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: The Isabella Stewart Gardner library pass allows you to get up to four tickets at $5 each. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted free. Your confirmation email provides a Promo Code (below barcode) needed to reserve your timed ticket with the Gardner Museum. PATRONS MUST PRESENT BOTH THE LIBRARY PASS AND THEIR RESERVED TIMED MUSEUM ADMISSION TICKETS FOR ENTRY INTO THE MUSUEM. Please plan ahead and make your reservation well in advance of your intended visit. When reserving Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum timed entry tickets online, you will: Go to https:/tnew.gardnermusuem.org/events Submit the promo code in the box at the top of the screen Select the visit date and time to enter Add desired number of tickets to the basket. The library pass allows you to get up to four tickets at $5 each (choose the Library Pass ticket type). Any additional tickets will be normally priced (Children under 18 are always free and should be reserved as child tickets) Login or create an account Finish checking out - discount is applied only at checkout THIS PASS IS VALID ONCE UNTIL 11/30/26 This library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum. If you have any issues reserving your tickets, call the Gardner Museum's Box Office at 617-278-5156. MUSUEM HOURS: WEEKDAYS 11 AM - 5 PM | WEEKENDS 10 AM - 5 PM | THURSDAYS 11 AM - 9 PM | CLOSED TUESDAYS The museum is closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day, Patriot's Day and Independence Day.

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: This single-use Library Partner Admission Pass admits up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. A discounted admission fee of $2 per person will be charged for anyone over the age of 2. Your confirmation e-mail will allow you to print out your pass or present it on your mobile device. Pass must be presented to Mass Audubon staff and is only valid for the date printed on your pass. Please visit https://www.massaudubon.org/get-outdoors for the latest information on sanctuary hours and closure dates due to special fundraising events or inclement weather. Mass Audubon does not guarantee access on any specific date/time if tickets are not available.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: DCR Parks pass allows one car unlimited day-use parking access to state park facilities that charge a parking fee. (Not valid for parking at Quabbin Reservoir, Sqantum Point, North Point, Draw Seven Commuter Lots or for camping.) You will need to pick up your pass at the Circulation Desk in the main lobby of the Library. We encourage you to visit www.mass.gov/orgs/department-of-conservation-recreation for a full listing of DCR locations and hours of operation before making your travel plans.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: ADMISSION INCREASE: Starting January 20, 2026 the adult admission price for MFA with a library discount will increase from $10 each to $15 each (youth prices will remain at $10 each) The Library Discount Pass admits 2 adults for $10 each (this includes general admission and special exhibitions). In addition, the pass admits up to 4 children ages 7-17 into special exhibitions for $10 each (general admission for 7-17 is regularly $10). PLEASE NOTE: If your plans are to visit the museum for a special exhibit, order the special exhibit ticket. The cost is $10 each and will include general admission to the museum. Children 6 and under are admitted free. Once reserved, you will receive an e-mail that contains a discount code. You can print the library pass with discount code or display it on your mobile device for use at the museum or you can use the discount code to make advance reservations on the museums website. Promo Code may not be combined with any other offers. Pass is not applicable towards discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses or any other public programs.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: This pass admits up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are always admitted for free. 1. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. 2. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone). 3. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase Exhibit Hall tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Hall passes in advance, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. daily).

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: This coupon allows 50% off admission for up to 4 people to the New England Aquarium. Reserve your date by clicking the Reserve a Pass button below. You will need the BFPL ID# provided on your coupon pass in the confirmation email. Your confirmation e-mail allows you to print coupon pass or display on your mobile device. Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth. Only applies to adult and child pricing. This is not a ticket Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. Review the Aquarium’s website at neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. Valid only for Aquarium admission. This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. Void if altered or copied. No cash value. Hours of operation are subject to change. Coupon pass may not be used in conjunction with school or group visits, nor be applied to Aquarium programs, Simons Theatre tickets, Whale Watch tickets or combination tickets. Always check with museum to verify open/close schedule.

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: The Library Pass to New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill allows a 50% admission discount for two people. Advance reservations are no longer required but are still available for those who would like to purchase tickets in advance. Once you reserve the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill pass you will receive an email that includes instructions to get the 50% discount. This pass requires you to pick up the discount pass from the library the day before your visit. This laminated membership pass needs to be returned to the library after your visit. First-time visitors can review admission information at: nebg.org/planyourvisit

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: The discount pass admits 2 adults at a reduced admission rate of $12.00 per person any day the museum is open. Children under 16 are always free. With the discount pass patrons may reserve discounted tickets at my.pem.org/events or by calling the PEM reservation line at 978-542-1511. Admission to Yin Yu Tang the Chinese House and special ticketed exhibitions may require a separate fee. Please make sure to review health and safety requirements before your visit.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: All Trustee properties are open and timed ticketing is in place for the more popular sites. Patrons will need to check with each site as to what is required to visit. See their website at https://thetrustees.org/ for more information. PLEASE NOTE: Crane's Beach Parking is NOT Available with this pass. DeCordova is partnered with the Trustees of Reservations. This pass admits patrons into DeCordova at a reduced admission for 2 adults and 2 children (under 17 yrs of age). For DeCordova, you must purchase timed tickets at https://thetrustees.org/place/decordova/ . PLEASE NOTE: DeCordova's inside galleries are currently closed for renovations. The sculpture park, cafe, and retail store remain open and public programs are active Reserve a date for your visit, then pick up the library discount pass at the Circulation Desk in the main lobby up to one week in advance of your reservation. Purchase your timed tickets at https://thetrustees.org/place/ and enter the code: GOPASS. You will need to bring both your purchased tickets and the pass from the library.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 6 people at a reduced admission of $9 per adult and $6 per child aged 2-12 yrs. Pass can be used at either the Franklin Park Zoo in Boston or the Stone Zoo in Stoneham. Pick up discount pass at the library either the day before or the day of your visit. Bring this pass to the Zoo ticket desk to receive admission discount (passes are not valid for Stone Zoo Zoolights or Franklin Park Zoo Boston Lights). For additional details on Zoo New England's policies and hours of operation please visit there website at zoonewengland.org . Please note: Zoo New England no longer accepts printable passes from confirmation email.

armenian-museum-of-america.description: With roots going back to the early 1970s, The Armenian Museum of America has occupied its Watertown location since 1988. The largest collection of Armenian cultural artifacts outside of Armenia, the 25,000 objects include five of the first printed bibles in Armenian from 1666, ancient Urartian artifacts, medieval manuscripts, K�tahya ceramics, textiles, liturgical objects, and contemporary art. With such a large collection, the museum estimates that only around 5% of its holding are on view at a given time, through rotating and permanent exhibits, including explorations of modern day Armenian-American culture, artworks by contemporary Armenian and Armenian-American artists, and an exhibit focusing on the Armenian Genocide. All galleries are wheelchair accessible, and there are HEPA Air Purifiers throughout. There is a gift shop featuring work by Armenian vendors.
armenian-museum-of-america.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
armenian-museum-of-america.benefits_text: The Armenian Museum of America has generously donated a limited number of guest passes to the Belmont Public Library. This pass discount provides free admission for 1 individual. This coupon pass must be picked up in person at the Reference desk.

boston-by-foot.description: Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston's rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes.
boston-by-foot.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-by-foot.benefits_text: Boston by Foot pass admits 4 people on any regularly scheduled tour from May 1st through October 31st. No co-pay is charged with the pass. Please reserve a Boston by Foot printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a timed-entry discounted admission ticket. You can enter the discount code into the discount code box on the Boston by Foot website (www.bostonbyfoot.org) when reserving tickets to tours in advance of the tour. First select the date you want to go by clicking "Calendar," then select the number of tickets. Once tickets are selected, enter the promo code found in your email in the discount code box. These passes are not valid on for annual events, Footloose on the Freedom Trail and Beacon with a Boo!

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Please note: The Boston Children's Museum hours change frequently. Please verify the date you wish to visit via their website before reserving your pass. Please reserve a Boston Children's Museum printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will need to show this pass, either printed or on your handheld device, for discounted entry to the museum. After receiving the printable museum pass from the library, you then reserve your visit online with Boston Children’s Museum on their page here, under "Discount Tickets: https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing/. Advance tickets/reservations are highly recommended, especially during weekends, holidays and school vacation weeks. The Boston Children's Museum pass admits 4 people. With the pass, the current co-pay is half price per person. The Museum is operating hours are from 9 AM to 4 PM.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. Pass Benefits This discount pass admits 3 visitors at 1/2 price admission per person; any additional visitors must pay the regular admission price. Your confirmation e-mail will allow you to print the pass or show it on your phone. Discount is only valid for the date printed on the Library Pass. Present the Library Discount Pass to Admissions staff when checking in and the discount will be applied to the cost of your visit.

gore-place.description: Gore Place is a historic 19th century country house located in Waltham, MA. Indoor and outdoor tours of the 50-acre estate—which boasts a magnificent Mansion, an authentic 1793 Carriage House, a working farm, and beautiful grounds—are available; check the website for the schedule.
