They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 4 people with a 50% discount. The current admission rates can be found on the New England Aquarium's website . Children aged 2 and under are always admitted for free. This pass does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. In June, July and August this pass is not available Saturday or Sunday.

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 guests at half price off general admission

new-england-quilt-museum.description: The New England Quilt Museum, part of the Lowell National Historic Park, is fittingly located not far from the old textile manufacturing plants that made the city famous during the industrial revolution. Since its opening in 1987, it has been committed to both craft and fine arts quilts, with a permanent collection, special exhibitions, a library and resource center, classrooms, and a gift shop.
new-england-quilt-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-quilt-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 people free of charge.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Pass allows you to purchase up to two tickets at the reduced rate of $12 each. The code you'll receive is for one time use only and is not combinable with any other discount.

plimoth-patuxet-museums.description: Plimoth Patuxet Museums offers a living history that tells the story of the English colonists who became known as “Pilgrims” and the Native Americans with whom they interacted. A big draw is the re-creation of the original settlement, populated with interpreters in period dress with whom one may interact that are based on actual historical figures. Native People from various nations in historically accurate dress provide information about the Wampanoag—the first tribe the Pilgrims encountered—at a home site based on how the group would have lived in the 17th century. In addition to the two settlements, the museum includes a visitor center, the Craft Center, barns that contain rare and heritage animal breeds, and a grist mill that manufactures cornmeal. Plymouth Patuxet Museums is also responsible for the replica of the Mayflower, the ship that brought the Pilgrims to the New World.
plimoth-patuxet-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
plimoth-patuxet-museums.benefits_text: This pass allows patrons to purchase a Heritage Pass , Combination Pass , or Plimoth Patuxet Only pass for up to six individuals at the reduced rate of $18 per adult; $12 per child ages 5-17. Not combinable with any other discount. Tickets must be purchased in person at the Museum in order to receive the discount. No online ticket sales.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: This pass is valid for one-time use at any of our 100+ properties that collect an admission fee. Pass holder will pay an admission fee equivalent to that of a Trustees Family Level membership (two adults & children 17 and under.)

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 5 people to the museum for free. This pass is valid for the museum only and not for the ship.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass allows up to 6 people to purchase discounted admission ($9.00/adult and $6.00/child) to the Stone Zoo or the Franklin Park Zoo. Library passes are good for daytime admission only, and not valid for Boston Lights or ZooLights.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Emailed voucher admits up to 4 people (adults or children) at half-price admission

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: This coupon code gives a 50% discount for the 202 Boston Harbor Ferry season for the following islands and dates: Georges Island May 15- June 15: Thursdays through Sundays June 16- September 1: every day Spectacle Island July 15 - September 1: every day September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays Peddocks Island June 15 - September 7: every day but Tuesdays September 10- October 12: Thursdays through Sundays

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: Free admission for up to 4 people. At least one 1 must bring proof of MA residence. This pass can be used at the following location: Harvard Museum of Natural History, The Peabody Museum of Archeology and Ethnology, The Harvard Museum of the Near East, or the The Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people at $5 admission per person. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted for free. In addition to presenting your coupon at the museum, you must reserve your tickets online at https://tnew.gardnermuseum.org/events Pass does not guarantee entry.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Pass must be returned at the Chelsea Public Library the day after use. Free parking for 1 vehicle at state park facilities that charge a parking fee.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Each promo code may be redeemed for up to two adult tickets ($15 each) and four youth tickets ($10 each). Children 6 and under are admitted for free. Promo codes are redeemable online, over the phone, or at any MFA ticket desk. Passes are not applicable toward discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses, or any other public programs. All prices are subject to change. Discount admission may not be combined with other offers. When planning your visit, please check www.mfa.org/visit for current Museum hours and early closings. The Museum is closed on New Year’s Day, Patriots’ Day, Fourth of July, Thanksgiving, and Christmas.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. 1. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. 2. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. 3. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass, and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or if you would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). 4. If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people (adults or children) at half price per person. Children 2 and under are free. Passes are not accepted on weekends from June-August. In the event of a closure, passes will be honored up to a week after the original printed date.

salem-witch-museum.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
salem-witch-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
salem-witch-museum.benefits_text: This is a physical pass that must be picked up and returned to the library. Discounted admission for up to 6 people; $13.75 for adults and $10.75 for children. Code on pass must be redeemed online and pass presented at the museum. Pass not available in October.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 6 people at $9/adult and $6/child. Pass may be used at either Stoneham Zoo or Franklin Park Zoo. Passes are not date-specific so plan for any day that works for you, up to the expiration date on the pass itself. You might consider going with another family on the same day and each family can get their own pass.

american-heritage-museum.description: The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the “War on Terror.” A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century.
american-heritage-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
american-heritage-museum.benefits_text: Pass Benefits: Half-price admission to the American Heritage Museum for up to six people per pass.

boston-athenaeum.description: Once one of the country's largest libraries, The Boston Athenaeum has been building their collection of books, manuscripts, art and artifacts since the early 1800s. A non-profit independent library, museum and cultural center, the Athenaeum has a circulating library of over half a million books from the 19th century to the present day. In addition, it houses ample special collections of rare books, authors' manuscripts, maps, and art, much of it pertaining to Boston's cultural history. The galleries display highlights of the permanent collections, as well as rotating exhibits focusing on local artists and history. Several dates a week are devoted to events: tours, lectures, and even concerts. Visit the website for the museum's hours, as well as a room-by-room breakdown of accessibility.
boston-athenaeum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
boston-athenaeum.benefits_text: This is a Day Membership pass which allows two people to visit the Boston Athenaeum during operating hours at no cost. Pass holders are welcome to experience the Athenaeum as a member for a day, enjoy access to 12 floors of inviting spaces, with WiFi, well-lit work tables, cozy nooks, and beautiful city views.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Half price admission for up to four people

concord-museum.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
concord-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
concord-museum.benefits_text: Each pass admits 4 guests at $5 off per person.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: This pass admits up to 3 visitors at 1/2 price admission per person ($9.50 per person); any additional visitors must pay the regular admission price. Children under one year of age are admitted free.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: BENEFITS Library Passes are $10 per ticket (discounted from the regular fee of $20 per ticket). One library pass will be valid for up to two admission tickets at the discounted price. Current pricing and visitor information: https://www.icaboston.org/visit Patrons under the age of 18 are free without the pass.

The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: - Each pass admits up to 4 adults for $5 each (General Admission only). - Children under 18 are admitted for free.

mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.description: Mass Audubon maintains about 100 wildlife sanctuaries in Massachusetts, more than half of which may be visited for walks on trails and visits to nature centers and museums. Among these is Drumlin Farm in Lincoln, a working farm with animals and crops, plus exhibits that include native wildlife, such as owls, foxes and deer. A shop on the premises sells items related to nature, such as bird feeders and binoculars.
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.benefits_text: This Library Partner Admission Pass admits up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary at $2.00 per person over the age of two.

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: This pass allows admittance for two guests at 50% off the general admission price.

orchard-house.benefits_text: Each pass admits 2 adults and up to 4 youth at half-price admission

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Each pass allows admission for 2 people for $12.00 per person Thursday through Mondays.

Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: This pass provides 50% off discount for up to 4 people. To use this past you MUST make a reservation on the museum's website. To do this: Visit: https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing/ Select "Discount Programs" Select "Purchase Discount Program Tickets" Select "Half Price Library Tickets" Select the correct date (must match date of the pass) Select the time slot Select the number of tickets (maximum 4) Select "Add to Cart" Select "Check out" and complete your payment You will be sent the tickets via email The day of your visit, please bring both the library coupon pass and the ticket from the Museum. Your coupon and tickets can be printed out or shown on a smartphone or other device. If you have problems ordering your museum ticket, email: Orders@BostonChildrensMuseum.org Hours of Operation The Museum is open from 9am to 4pm Wednesday through Monday. It is closed on Tuesdays. This is a printable pass. You will receive the pass in your email or library staff can print it for you after you have made a reservation.

the-childrens-piazza.address: 83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960
the-childrens-piazza.description: Located in Peabody, MA, The Children's Piazza is a space is designed for young children—aged 0 to 6—to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry.
the-childrens-piazza.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-childrens-piazza.benefits_text: This pass provides 50% off admission for one family/same household per day. The pass does not cover members of the group who are not immediate family/household. Weekends (Saturdays) and the following holidays are excluded: Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Eve, New Year's Day, and the 4th of July. Please visit The Children's Piazza website (thechildrenspiazza.com) to make a reservation prior to your visit. Reservations required for entry. Pass only valid on date reserved. The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the Children's Piazza. Library staff may print this pass for you upon request.

griffin-museum-of-photography.description: Opened in 1992, the Griffin Museum of Photography presents exhibitions, programs and lectures that support their mission of encouraging a broader understanding and appreciation of the art of photography. Three galleries are dedicated to changing exhibitions of photography by well-known, emerging, and student photographers. (Three additional satellite galleries can be found in various locations in the Boston area.)
griffin-museum-of-photography.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.

At least one member of the party must be a MA resident. This pass may not be combined with other offers. This pass is not for group, institutional, or corporate visits. HMSC Museums: Harvard Museum of Natural History Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the library.

historic-new-england.address: 141 Cambridge Street, Administrative Offices: Otis House, Boston, MA 02114
historic-new-england.description: Historic New England is a historic preservation organization which owns and operates historic house museums and properties open to the public throughout most of New England. The majority of the 36 properties are in Massachusetts, but locations exist in all NE states but Vermont. These include Castle Tucker (Wiscasset, ME), the Jackson House (Portsmouth, NH), the Spencer-Pierce-Little Farm, which (Newbury, MA), Roseland Cottage (Woodstock, CT), the Otis House (Boston, MA), the Lyman Estate (Waltham, MA), Gropius House (Lincoln, MA), and the Arnold House (Lincoln, RI). Styles represented include Federal, Colonial Revival, Victorian, Queen Anne, Arts & Crafts, and Modern, among many others. The website provides much detail, including directions, contact information, and history of each property.
historic-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
historic-new-england.benefits_text: Pass allows free admission for two adults and their children and or grandchildren under age 18 to tour one of historic New England's thirty-eight sites. Pre booking tours in encouraged using the promo code on the back of the pass. Pass does not have to be returned to the Library.

In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. The discount only works for up to 4 Adult priced tickets and any additional ticket will be normally priced (Children under 18 are always free and should be reserved as child tickets). john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.benefits_text: UPDATE: NO PASS IS NEEDED JUST SHOW YOUR LIBRARY CARD Present your physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive a 50% discount on the cardholder's admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and the visitor must present a Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. No pass is needed. Visitors aged 12 and under receive free admission. Please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Services Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600. No reservation is required and no pass from the library is needed. Simply present your library card at admissions to receive the 50% discount. All members of your group ages 13+ needs to present a library card to receive the discount.

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: This one-day Library Partner Admission Pass admits up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. With this pass, admission is discounted to $2 per visitor over the age of 2.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Each promo code use provides up to two adults and four youths (7–17) with $15 admission (general and special exhibition) per person. Tickets to the MFA are always free for visitors under seven years old. This is a printable pass. You will receive the pass in your email or library staff can print it for you after you have made a reservation. BRING THIS PASS TO THE ADMISSIONS DESK (either printed or on your device) at the museum to have the discount applied.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library. This is a printable pass. You will receive the pass in your email or library staff can print it for you after you have made a reservation.

naismith-memorial-basketball-hall-of-fam.museum_name: Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame
naismith-memorial-basketball-hall-of-fam.address: 1000 Hall of Fame Avenue, Springfield, MA 01105
naismith-memorial-basketball-hall-of-fam.description: The Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame, located in Springfield, MA, was established in 1959. The 40,000 sq. ft. museum is dedicated to the history of basketball and maintains a hall of fame dedicated to the most talented players of the game. Interactive exhibits share the spotlight with skills challenges, live clinics, and shooting contests.
naismith-memorial-basketball-hall-of-fam.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
naismith-memorial-basketball-hall-of-fam.benefits_text: Discounted admission for up to 6 visitors. Library Adult $20, Library Senior/Student $17, Library Youth $15

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: - This coupon allows 50% off standard admission to the New England Aquarium. - Only applies to adult and child pricing. - This is not a ticket. - Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. - Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. - Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. - Valid only for Aquarium admission. - This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. - Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. - Void if altered or copied. No cash value. - Hours of operation are subject to change.

north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: The reserved pass allows for 50% off admission for a family of five (adults/children); it can either be presented on a mobile device or as a print-out to receive the discounted admission rate in-person. Passes will be emailed to reserving patrons, without the need to come to the library to pick up the pass! Please contact the library to reserve this pass if you do not have an email address. Advanced ticket purchase is strongly recommended but not required. To purchase tickets online, visit https://www.nschildrensmuseum.org/visit/buy-tickets/, click on "Buy Tickets" and select your preferred admission. Your library pass will only be honored on the date you reserved. If the museum reaches capacity, visitors will be let in on a one in, one out basis.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 people for $12 per person for general admission only. Reservations for your ticket may be made in advance by calling the museum or by going to pem.org/tickets Yin Yu Tang Chinese House and other special exhibits require additional admission fees. This is a printable pass. You will receive the pass in your email or library staff can print it for you after you have made a reservation.

salem-witch-museum.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
salem-witch-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
salem-witch-museum.benefits_text: This pass allows the bearer a discounted admission rate ($13.50 for adults and seniors, $11.25 for children ages 3-14) for up to six visitors. Please visit www.salemwitchmuseum.com to purchase tickets, using the code on the physical museum pass. Discount cannot be applied after, nor can a refund be administered upon visiting the museum for tickets purchased without the discount. Pass must be presented to receive tickets. If you can not provide the library pass, you will be charged full admission fee. This is a physical pass that you must pick up from and return to the library. Please return by 9 am the day after the Museum Pass is used. The pass may be returned via the book drop. The pass is not valid the month of October.

wenham-museum.description: Wenham Museum is a history museum celebrating the treasures of childhood and New England family life across four centuries, with an emphasis on the North Shore. The museum's collections feature a world-renowned doll collection and dollhouses, costumes and textiles, model trains, antique toys, historic photographs, and the interactive Family Discovery Gallery. Admission includes a tour of the historic Claflin-Gerrish-Richards House, built at the end of the 17th century. In addition, there are rotating special programs and exhibitions. A gift shop is on the premises.
wenham-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
wenham-museum.benefits_text: This discount pass is good for 4 general admissions at half price. Reservations do not need to be made in advance. *This pass is for general admission only and special programs the museum offers are not included in the pass ( this included Kids' Countdown to New Year's Eve (on New Year's Eve Day), Fairy Festival (TBD), or other programs that have special pricing)* The Wenham Museum is not open Easter, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas, Day or New Year's Day. We are not open on Mondays - except for MLK Day and President's Day. During mid May - Labor Day, we are not open on Sundays in the Summer. For admission for this pass, simply show the confirmation email date on your phone at the front desk of the museum or bring a printed out copy. Beginning on January 2nd, our general admission rates will be changing. Visitors with museum passes from your libraries, will still receive half-off of their admissions. If you mention admission rates in any of correspondence with your patrons, please be state our new rates starting on January 2nd. Adults: $15 Seniors & Children: $10 (Ages 2-18) Children Under 2: Free (this is a change from our current free age)

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass admits 6 people to Franklin Park Zoo (Boston) or Stone Zoo (Stoneham). Discounted rate is $9 per adult; $6 per child. Free admission for children under 2. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the library. No timed entry tickets necessary.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Admission is HALF price for up to four (4) people.

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: The Boston Harbor Islands Discounted Passes offer 50% off up to 4 ferry tickets to visit Spectacle or Georges Islands. The code can be used throughout the entire 2025 season, which is listed below: Georges Island May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays June 16 - September 1: every day Spectacle Island July 15 - September 1: every day September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays Peddocks Island from Hingham June 15 - September 7: every day BUT Tuesdays September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture Complimentary Museum Pass allows for free admission to all four HMSC museums. Terms & Conditions Limit of four visitors per coupon. One member of the party must be a MA resident. Not for a group, institutional, or corporate visits. May not be combined with other offers. Nontransferrable.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Each unique discount code provides up to four (4) people with $5 admission per person—children under the age of 18 are always admitted free. Patron's must utilize the discount code online to make a reservation for the museum. This library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum. If you have any issues reserving your tickets, you can call the Gardner Museum's Box Office at 617 278 5156.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Patrons may check out up to one pass for the Massachusetts Department of Conservation and Recreation per month. This is a physical pass that must be picked up from the library at which you made the reservation. The pass must be returned to the library you borrowed it from within 3 days of it's use.

In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Children 6 and under are admitted free. Each pass allows admission for 2 adults at $10 each (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7–17 at $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for children 7–17 for general admission, currently $10. Please note the MFA is increasing prices for Institutional Pass discounted admission tickets for visit dates beginning January 20, 2026. Going forward, each pass will allow admission for 2 adults at $15 each and up to 4 children ages 7–17 at $10 each.

Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. Void if altered or copied. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: The PEM Library Pass allows admission for up to two (2) adult visitors with the reduced admission rate of $12 per person. Children age 16 and under are always admitted free. Patrons must use their unique discount code to reserve tickets at the discounted rate. The Library Pass does not guarantee admission to the Peabody Essex Museum. Admission to special ticketed exhibitions may require a separate fee.

trustees-of-the-reservations.description: The Trustees of the Reservations preserve and protect more than 100 special properties of exceptional scenic, historic, and ecological value—nearly 25,000 acres—all around Massachusetts. From Crane's Beach in Ipswich and World's End in Hingham to The Old Manse in Concord and Fruitlands Museum in Harvard, visitors to Trustees sites enjoy local history and nature in its various forms: beaches, meadows, forests, swamps and more.
trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: GO Passes allow for one-time use as admission for two adults and children under 18 to any of the 100+ Trustees properties. GO Pass admission is equivalent to a Trustees Family Membership admission price at Trustees properties that charge an admission fee. GO Passes are valid for admission only; not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, Inns, Campgrounds, at special events or programs, on equipment rentals, or in place of any permits or passes. To use GO Passes with a Trustees site that requires timed entry, you will need to enter the promo code listed on the physical pass. Passes are available at www.thetrustees.org/passes

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: Pass holders will receive free admission to the USS Constitution museum. One pass allows free admission for up to 5 people per day. One pass per party, please.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Admits 6 people at $9.00 per Adult and $6.00 per Child. Under age 2 is free. Valid for general daytime admission to Franklin Park Zoo or Stone Zoo only. Not valid for Boston Lights or ZooLights . No reservation required.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Up to 4 persons at half price; infants under 12 months admitted free but still need a ticket.

the-danforth.description: The Danforth is an art museum and school that opened in Framingham in 1975. After a closure of three years, the museum reopened in 2019 in a newly renovated location on Centre Common. It has six galleries, one dedicated to its collection of American art from the 1800s up to the present; a second containing the work of the African-American female sculptor Meta Vaux Warrick Fuller; and four used as spaces for changing exhibitions. The museum's permanent collection includes work by Gilbert Stuart, Charles Sprague Pearce, Eastman Johnson, Albert Bierstadt, and Thomas Hart Benton, as well as work by the Boston Expressionists and contemporary artists such as Faith Ringgold, Richard Yarde, Barbara Grad, Andrew Stevovich, and Jason Berger.
the-danforth.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
the-danforth.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 persons free of charge

davis-farmland.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
davis-farmland.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
davis-farmland.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 6 guests to Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze with $10 off the at-the-gate admission rate.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: Up to three half price admissions.

garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.benefits_text: Admits 2 persons; adults at 1/2 price, children (5-17) $1.00

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Pass allows free daytime parking for one carload to most Massachusetts State Parks, State Forests, or State Reservations that charge a parking fee.
