Present pass upon arrival to receive discount. -Special and evening events are not eligible for this discount.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: (PRINTABLE) 1 pass admits 4 people at half price admission. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY. After receiving a library discount coupon from the library, patrons must then reserve their visit online with Boston Children’s Museum. Due to safety measures walk-ins are not allowed.

concord-museum.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
concord-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
concord-museum.benefits_text: (RETURNABLE) Each pass admits 4 people at $5.00 off regular admission. Children under 6 are always free. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY.

the-danforth.description: The Danforth is an art museum and school that opened in Framingham in 1975. After a closure of three years, the museum reopened in 2019 in a newly renovated location on Centre Common. It has six galleries, one dedicated to its collection of American art from the 1800s up to the present; a second containing the work of the African-American female sculptor Meta Vaux Warrick Fuller; and four used as spaces for changing exhibitions. The museum's permanent collection includes work by Gilbert Stuart, Charles Sprague Pearce, Eastman Johnson, Albert Bierstadt, and Thomas Hart Benton, as well as work by the Boston Expressionists and contemporary artists such as Faith Ringgold, Richard Yarde, Barbara Grad, Andrew Stevovich, and Jason Berger.
the-danforth.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-danforth.benefits_text: (PRINTABLE) This pass admits 4 guests free of charge. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.

garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.

Reservation and adherance to vaccination policy is required.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.

Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: (PRINTABLE) · This pass admits up to four people (adult, senior, child, or college) at a cost of $20 each. · Children under age 3 are always admitted free. · This is not a ticket. · Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. . Advance reservations are not available for Library Pass holders. Patrons will be admitted regardless of if the Aquarium is sold out to the general public. · Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. · Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. · Valid only for Aquarium admission. · This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. · Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. · Void if altered or copied. No cash value. · Hours of operation are subject to change. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: (RETURNABLE) Pass admits two guests at half-price. Children 3 and under are admitted free of charge. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY. Tickets can be purchased at the door, but reservations are recommended for busy days. To reserve tickets in advance, you will need to use the discount code LIBDI and then get the physical pass from the library. Access the Ticket Reservation System at purchase.nebg.org

new-england-quilt-museum.description: The New England Quilt Museum, part of the Lowell National Historic Park, is fittingly located not far from the old textile manufacturing plants that made the city famous during the industrial revolution. Since its opening in 1987, it has been committed to both craft and fine arts quilts, with a permanent collection, special exhibitions, a library and resource center, classrooms, and a gift shop.
new-england-quilt-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-quilt-museum.benefits_text: (RETURNABLE) Pass admits 2. Tuesday - Saturday, 10:00 - 4:00. Closed Sundays and Mondays. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: (PRINTABLE) Each pass admits 2 visitors for $12.00 per person. The museum is open Modays 10-5, Thursdays 10-5, Friday & Saturdays 10-8, and Sundays 10-5. Children 16 and under are admitted free. Not valid for admission to Yin Yu Tang Chinese House (additional $6.00). FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY. To receive library discount, patrons must first reserve their library museum pass. Then visit https://pem.org/tickets to reserve a timed entry to the museum and any events/ exhibits you wish to attend. Enter promo code LIB268183 in the upper right corner and click submit. This will bring you to the page where you select the date/time, general admission, and events/exhibits you wish to attend. Once that is completed, the next page asks Who Will Attend? Make sure to select Library Discount (do not select Adult) For Covid-19 restrictions and protocols please visit https://www.pem.org/open/your-health-safety .

plimoth-patuxet-museums.description: Plimoth Patuxet Museums offers a living history that tells the story of the English colonists who became known as “Pilgrims” and the Native Americans with whom they interacted. A big draw is the re-creation of the original settlement, populated with interpreters in period dress with whom one may interact that are based on actual historical figures. Native People from various nations in historically accurate dress provide information about the Wampanoag—the first tribe the Pilgrims encountered—at a home site based on how the group would have lived in the 17th century. In addition to the two settlements, the museum includes a visitor center, the Craft Center, barns that contain rare and heritage animal breeds, and a grist mill that manufactures cornmeal. Plymouth Patuxet Museums is also responsible for the replica of the Mayflower, the ship that brought the Pilgrims to the New World.
plimoth-patuxet-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
plimoth-patuxet-museums.benefits_text: Pass is valid for up to 6 guests to each of Plimoth Patuxet Museum's living history sites, to purchase a Heritage Pass at the discounted admission rates of $18 per adult and $12 per child. (Regularly $46 and $29) Library ID#52591

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: (COUPON) Pass is valid for one-time use at any of the 118 Trustees properties that collect an admission fee, including the deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum. Pass holder will pay an admission fee equivalent to that of a Trustees Family level membership. Two adults and children under 18 included in admission. Pass is not valid for use at Trustees parking kiosks, stores, cafes Inns, Campgrounds, at special events and programs, or in place of any permits or passes. To use GO Passes with a site that requires timed passes, enter the promo code " GOPASS " when securing a timed ticket. For a complete list of properties go to http://thetrustees.org/ FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: (PRINTABLE) Pass admits up to 5 people with free admission to the USS Constitution Museum. Museum is open daily April 1 - October 31 9:00-6:00, and November 1 - March 31 10:00-5:00. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY

worcester-art-museum.description: The Worcester Art Museum is recognized for its 35,000-piece collection of paintings, sculpture, decorative arts, photography, prints, drawings and new media created over the last 5 millennia. Works include those by El Greco, Monet, Rembrandt, Gauguin, van Gogh, Renoir, Cézanne, Picasso, Copley, Gilbert Stuart, Paul Revere, and Nam June Paik. Special exhibitions showcase the masterworks, seldom-seen gems, and important works on loan—including some contemporary art. Of note are the floor mosaics from the ancient city of Antioch (Syria) in the entrance, the Japanese print collection, and the “Arms and Armor” collection. There is a café, museum shop, and library on the premises.
worcester-art-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
worcester-art-museum.benefits_text: (RETURNABLE) Pass admits 2 adults at 50% off admission price. Children 17 and under are always free. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: (COUPON) Pass admits 6 people at $9.00 per adult and $6.00 per child. FOR NEEDHAM RESIDENTS ONLY.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person.

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: Admits 50% off up to 4 ferry tickets from Boston to Spectacle Island and Georges Island, or from Hingham to Peddocks Island. This is a SEASONAL pass available mid-May through early October. Please CHECK SCHEDULE on website before booking. Not all islands are open every day. Specific dates and islands for the current 2026 season are at https://www.bostonharborislands.org/ferryschedule/

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: Admits 3 visitors at half price admission per person to the Discovery Museum as well as Discovery Woods. Any additional visitors must pay the regular admission price.

ecotarium.description: The EcoTarium is a unique indoor-outdoor museum in Worcester, MA. Set in an urban oasis, the museum offers a chance to walk through the treetops, take a multimedia journey through the galaxy at a digital planetarium, meet wildlife, stroll nature trails, and get hands-on with family-friendly exhibits.
ecotarium.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
ecotarium.benefits_text: Admits up to 2 adults and 2 children in one vehicle at a 50% discount for general admission. Not valid for tickets to the Alden Digital Planetarium, the Explorer Express Train, or special ticketed events.

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people at half off per person. Admission to the Museum of Natural History also allows visitors entry to the Peabody Museum of Archaeology and Ethnology (located in the same complex).

historic-newton.description: Historic Newton manages two museums and three historic burying grounds. The first of the museums —the Jackson Homestead and Museum—is located in a federal style house built in 1809, which was a stop on the Underground Railroad. Exhibits there focus on the history of food, farming, and family life; slavery and anti-slavery; and notable people and events in Newton. The second museum—the Durant-Kenrick House and Grounds—is home to a farmhouse built in 1734 with period rooms, interactive games and puzzles. The grounds feature fruit and ornamental tree varieties from the nursery of the original owners. Please refer to the website for the schedules of the museums, since they differ slightly.
historic-newton.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
historic-newton.benefits_text: Admits 4 adults and any children for free to the Jackson Homestead or the Durant-Kenrick House & Grounds.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: Admits 2 people at $10 per person (special exhibition + general admission).

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Admits 4 people at $5.00 per person (General Admissions ONLY). Persons under 18 not included in admission count and always admitted free.

larz-anderson-auto-museum.description: The museum is located in an imposing carriage house built in 1888 (listed on the National Register of Historic Places) on the grounds of beautiful Larz Anderson Park. In addition to 14 autos from “America's Oldest Car Collection,” originally assembled by the Andersons, the museum features revolving annual exhibits, horse-drawn carriages and sleighs, a vintage bicycle collection, and changing lawn exhibits of show cars that typically include Italian, German, British, American, Custom, Tuner, Alternative Fuels, Trucks, Hot Rods and European Motorcycles from May to October. (Check the website for the schedule.)
larz-anderson-auto-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
larz-anderson-auto-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people for free.

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people at $2.00 per person to any Mass Audubon Wildlife Sanctuary. This includes access to Drumlin Farm, both a working farm and wildlife sanctuary. MONDAYS: many paid sanctuaries are closed. Please check the hours of your intended destination before booking.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Each pass admits one vehicle for free to Massachusetts State Parks. Patrons may book up to 2 passes per day.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Admits 2 adults at $15 each and up to 4 children (ages 7-17) at $10 each - applies to both special exhibition + general admission Free for children under 6 years of age.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls (General Admission). Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.

new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Admits 4 people at 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium for only adult and child pricing. Valid only for Aquarium admission.

In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)

west-suburban-ymca.description: The West Suburban YMCA has two locations in Newton, MA. The Church St. YMCA (276 Church St.) features two indoor pools, an outdoor playground, an indoor track, cardio deck, full basketball court, a men's locker room with whirlpool and showers, a women's locker room with steam shower, a sauna, and private changing rooms with showers. The Wells Ave. YMCA (135 Wells Ave.) features a 4-lane 25-yard lap pool; four multi-purpose courts for basketball, volleyball, and pickleball; a fitness center; a cardio deck; a work lounge; and men's and women's locker rooms with showers. For schedules, please consult the website.
west-suburban-ymca.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
west-suburban-ymca.benefits_text: This Coupon pass admits one household and is good for a 1-week voucher of free access Newton's West Suburban YMCA. (Valid at both the Church St. and Wells Ave locations.) Pickup up to one week in advance. Redeem the coupon at the YMCA within 1 week of date written on coupon. You will receive 1 week of free access beginning the date you redeem at the YMCA. Each household may check out a YMCA pass once per calendar year. Check the Pool, Gym, and Group Exercise Classes Schedule here .

Works include those by El Greco, Monet, Rembrandt, Gauguin, van Gogh, Renoir, Cézanne, Picasso, Copley, Gilbert Stuart, Paul Revere, and Nam June Paik. Special exhibitions showcase the masterworks, seldom-seen gems, and important works on loan—including some contemporary art. Of note are the floor mosaics from the ancient city of Antioch (Syria) in the entrance, the Japanese print collection, and the “Arms and Armor” collection. There is a café, museum shop, and library on the premises.

The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.

They are not applicable toward discounts on special events, shopping, dining, concerts, lectures, or any other public programs. PATRONS MUST PRESENT BOTH THIS LIBRARY PASS AND THEIR RESERVED TIMED MUSEUM ADMISSION TICKETS FOR ENTRY INTO THE MUSEUM.

john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.benefits_text: Present your physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive a 50% discount on the cardholder's admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and the visitor must present a Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. No pass is needed. Visitors aged 12 and under receive free admission. Please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Services Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: The MassParks Pass entitles the bearer to free parking for one vehicle (excluding buses or vans) at over 50 facilities in the Massachusetts State Parks system that charge a day-use parking fee, with the exception of camping and excluding Quabbin Reservoir, Squantum Point, NorthPoint, or Draw Seven Commuter Lots. Check out www.mass.gov/dcr for a full listing of MassParks locations. Once you have reserved this pass, you may pick it up on the day before OR day of your reservation. This pass is returnable and must be returned the day of its use, as other patrons may be waiting to use it. Please return to the outside bookdrop.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Reserving this pass gives you access to a promo code which can be used for discounted admission. Each promo code provides a discount for: 2 adults at $15 each (general admission & special exhibition) up to 4 children ages 7–17 at $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for children 7–17 for general admission, currently $10. Children age six and younger are free Patrons who reserve tickets in advance online with the promo code will receive an e-mail with their admission ticket(s) attached and need to present their ticket(s) when they arrive at the Museum, preferably on a phone or device, but tickets printed on paper are accepted. Promo codes and library passes are not applicable toward discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses, or any other public programs. All prices are subject to change. Discount admission may not be combined with other offers.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass, and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or if you would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: Library ID: 57959 This coupon allows 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium. Only applies to adult and child pricing. This is not a ticket. Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. Valid only for Aquarium admission. This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. Void if altered or copied. No cash value. Hours of operation are subject to change.

north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: E-coupon Pass admits up to five at 50% off Printed or digital pass MUST be presented at the museum to receive the discount, even if purchased online.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Promo code that entitles you to a maximum of two general museum admission tickets at a reduced fee of $12 per person

trustees-of-the-reservations.description: The Trustees of the Reservations preserve and protect more than 100 special properties of exceptional scenic, historic, and ecological value—nearly 25,000 acres—all around Massachusetts. From Crane's Beach in Ipswich and World's End in Hingham to The Old Manse in Concord and Fruitlands Museum in Harvard, visitors to Trustees sites enjoy local history and nature in its various forms: beaches, meadows, forests, swamps and more.
trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: This pass must be picked up at the library. The Trustees GO Pass: is valid for admission only; it is not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, inns, campgrounds, special events and programs, on any equipment rental, or in place of any other permits or passes allows free or reduced admission at the Family Level Membership price at Trustees properties that charge an admission fee To use with a site that requires timed passes, please enter the promo code (printed on coupon) when securing a timed ticket. See www.thetrustees.org/passes for more details. Please note: Not every Trustees property charges an admission fee, and some locations require advanced ticketing. For information about the admission at each property, please visit TheTrustees.Org.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 5 people for free to the USS Constitution Museum. The USS Constitution Museum and the Ship are operated separately and do not share the same hours of operation. Security procedures are required to visit the Ship, but not the Museum. The Ship's hours and security information are subject to change at any time. Visit USS Constitution’s website for more information on visiting the Ship. Before visiting the USS Constitution Museum, please review their reopening policies here.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Each pass admits maximum of 6 people at $9 per adult and $6 per child. Children under 2 are admitted free. Valid for general daytime admission to Franklin Park Zoo or Stone Zoo only. Tickets are non-refundable, have no cash value, cannot be combined with other offers or discounts and cannot be replaced if lost or stolen. Not valid for ZooLights or Boston Lights.

the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.museum_name: The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.description: The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models.
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire.benefits_text: Pick up a paper pass at the Flint Memorial Library main circulation desk. Library Pass: Free entry for up to 4 people. Additional members in party must pay appropriate admission fee; kids 5 and under, FREE! The Museum is staffed by volunteers of the New Hampshire Aviation Historical Society and all admission fees and donations support exhibits, education programs, operations and maintenance.

boston-by-foot.description: Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston's rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes.
boston-by-foot.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-by-foot.benefits_text: Each pass provides free admission for up to 6 on Boston By Foot's public walking tours. Please consult www.bostonbyfoot.org for tour details; pre-registration is required for all tours. Reserve this pass to receive discount code. Not valid on private tours, water-based tours, or the two annual celebration tours (Beacon Hill with a BOO! & Footloose on the Freedom Trail.) Currently there is no coupon to pick up for Boston by Foot.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person. This is an e-ticket, valid only for one day. You can either print it out and take it with you or display the pass on your phone or device. Either way, please be sure that the pass barcode is visible.

the-butterfly-place.description: A winding pathway through an atrium filled with flowers, koi fish, and quail allows visitors to walk among butterflies from all over the world. Staff members are available for questions and “show and tell” demonstrations. A continuously running video explains the butterfly life cycle, and exhibits enable visitors to observe eggs, caterpillars, cocoons and chrysalides with information about each species. A gift shop is on the premises. There is a public picnic area and free parking is available.
the-butterfly-place.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-butterfly-place.benefits_text: Paper pass grants one free admission. You do not need to purchase tickets ahead of time or make a reservation unless you have 15 or more people and ticket sales end at 3:30 PM.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: This library sponsored pass admits up to three people at half price discount. Additional people in your party pay full price. Pass must be picked up at the library and returned by 10 AM the following morning.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: Admits 2 people at $10.00 per person. Admission is free for children 18 and under.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: This pass admits 4 visitors at $5 per person. Under 18 years admitted free. See website for latest hours, holidays and closings. Tickets are purchased online, but you must bring the library discounted pass with you.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: This pass allows free parking for one vehicle. Day Pass must be picked up at the library and returned by 10am the following morning.

In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 people. Admission is $10 per person. NOTE: This is a virtual pass with a promo code that does not need to be picked up from the library.

In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.

No cash value.

A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email) Admits up to 5 people at 50% off regular admission. Ages 12 months and under are free. To obtain discounted admission in person at the museum please present either a printed pass or on a handheld device. Visit https://www.nschildrensmuseum.org/visit/buy-tickets/ for more information. PASSES CANNOT BE USED ON SATURDAYS

It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass. Each pass admits up to 2 patrons at $12 per person. You must use the promo code provided in the loan instructions to reserve your pass.

sandmagination.description: Sandmagination is home to a number of sandboxes at the North Shore Mall in Peabody, MA. Aimed at children 10 and under, the sandboxes provide a hands-on, play-based learning experience, with STEAM-inspired activities like construction and tool usage. Toys and space are sanitized “constantly,” and the box is filled with allergen-free sand. There are also air purifiers onsite. While your child is playing, you can visit the café for coffee and free Wi-FI. Sandmagination is ADA-compliant. Check the website for hours, FAQs, or to sign the online waiver.
sandmagination.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
sandmagination.benefits_text: The SANDMAGINATION discount code admits up to (6) Adults/Children at 50% off on a Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, or Sunday. A discount code will be sent to your email address.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: The Trustees GO Pass: -can be used at over 100 Trustees of Reservation properties -covers two adults plus all children (or grandchildren) up to 18 years old -is valid for admission only; it is not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, inns, campgrounds, special events and programs, on any equipment rental, or in place of any other permits or passes -allows free or reduced admission at the Family Level Membership price Not every Trustees property charges an admission. For information about the admission at each property, please visit TheTrustees.Org.

the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: One digital pass allows free admission for up to 5 people per day. No advance tickets or reservations are required for entry, and admission will be on a first come, first served basis.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass admits 6 people at $9.00 per adult and $6.00 per child. Pass is NOT accepted for ZooLights. Your pass must be picked up at the library.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Please check Boston Children's Museum website for updated information before visiting. . Pass admits up to 4 people at half price. Children under 12 months free This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email. Advance tickets/reservations are highly recommended, especially on weekends, holidays, school vacation weeks.

the-childrens-piazza.address: 83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960
the-childrens-piazza.description: Located in Peabody, MA, The Children's Piazza is a space is designed for young children—aged 0 to 6—to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry.
the-childrens-piazza.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-childrens-piazza.benefits_text: This pass provides 50% off regular admission for one family (immediate family/household only). Weekends and the following holidays are excluded: Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Eve, New Year's Day, and the 4th of July. Please visit The Children's Piazza website (thechildrenspiazza.com) to make a reservation prior to your visit. Reservations required for entry. Pass only valid on date reserved. The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the Children's Piazza. Library staff may print this pass for you upon request. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

davis-farmland.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
davis-farmland.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
davis-farmland.benefits_text: Admission tickets are purchased at the gate with library pass. This pass admits up to 6 guests to Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze with a savings of $10 OFF the admission rate per person. Davis Farmland Discovery Farm is open April 16th-November 1st - dates and rates change with the season. Davis Farmland and Davis Mega Maze are only open WEATHER PERMITTING. ALWAYS call or check the web before heading out. Visit DavisFarmland.com for the most up-to-date operating schedule. NOTICE: Adults MUST be accompanied by at least 1 child 12 years or younger when visiting Davis Farmland. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Library.

the-house-of-seven-gables.description: The House of the Seven Gables, one of the oldest surviving timber-framed mansion houses in continental North America, was the setting of Nathaniel Hawthorne's famous 1851 novel of the same name. The historic site which includes the house (a National Historic Landmark District) includes a guided tour of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion (the House of the Seven Gables) and visits to the Nathaniel Hawthorne Birthplace, the Colonial Revival seaside gardens, and the Counting House, with a maritime discovery zone called “Kids' Cove at The Gables.” (An audio tour of the gardens, grounds, and waterfront is available through the UniGuide App.) Lectures and seasonal living history labs are scheduled throughout the year, and a changing annual exhibition examines various aspects of the institution's 350 years of history. There is a gift shop on the premises. The website has virtual field trips and interactive exhibitions that can provide helpful background information before a visit.
the-house-of-seven-gables.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-house-of-seven-gables.benefits_text: Library card must be in good standing to book a pass. Please check the House of Seven Gables website at 7gables.org for any updates before visiting. Pass admits 4 people at half price. Currently the Gardens and Grounds are open and tours of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion are available. The Museum shop is also open during operating hours. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch library.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Please check Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum website at gardnermuseum.org/visit/know-before-you-go for updates before visiting. Library passes must be redeemed online ( https://www.gardnermuseum.org/visit ) to reserve a timed ticket. Advance tickets for timed entry are required . Pass admits up to 4 people at $5.00 per person. Children 17 and under admitted free. Museum is closed on Tuesdays. Holiday closings: New Year's Day, Patriot's Day (April), Independence Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas. Passes are valid only for general admission and do not entitle participants to Gardner museum member benefits. They are not applicable toward discounts on special events, shopping, dining, concerts, lectures, or any other public and member programs. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. With pass, admission is discounted to $2 per visitor over the age of 2. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch library.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Provides free parking for 1 vehicle (excluding buses and vans) for day-use parking in state parks within MA Dept. of Conservation and Recreation. MassParks Pass is not valid for Lynch Park in Beverly or Crane's Beach in Ipswich. Most parks are open year round; check the web site for details: http://www.mass.gov/eea/agencies/dcr/massparks/places-to-go This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch Library.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Please check Museum of Fine Arts website for any updates before visiting. General admission tickets are available in person or in advance online at: mfa.org/tickets, or by phone. A library pass will not be accepted for admission into the Museum. Patrons can use the promo code ( given when you reserve your pass ) to reserve discounted admission tickets ahead of the reservation date. Patrons receive an e-mail with their admission ticket(s) and need to present their ticket(s) when they arrive at the Museum, preferably on a phone or device, but tickets printed on paper are accepted. A physical library pass will not be used for admission. Promo codes and library passes are not applicable toward discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses, or any other public programs. Pass admits 2 adults at $15 each (general and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7-17 at $10 (general or special exhibition). This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: Please check Museum of Science website for updated information before visiting. This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or if you would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily). If you are unable to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.

No cash value. · Hours of operation are subject to change. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Please purchase your ticket online. Use the Library Museum Pass pricing level to receive 50% off admission discount for a family of up to 5 adults/children. For hours and questions, call the NSCM at 978-538-5765 or visit www.nschildrensmuseum.org. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Please check Peabody Essex Museum website for updated information before visiting. Tickets bought in advance are encouraged at pem.org/tickets or by calling 978-542-1511. Pass admits 2 people for $12 per person for general admission only. Children under age 16 free general admission. Salem Residents free general admission. Yin Yu Tang Chinese House and other special exhibits require additional admission fees. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

plimoth-patuxet-museums.description: Plimoth Patuxet Museums offers a living history that tells the story of the English colonists who became known as “Pilgrims” and the Native Americans with whom they interacted. A big draw is the re-creation of the original settlement, populated with interpreters in period dress with whom one may interact that are based on actual historical figures. Native People from various nations in historically accurate dress provide information about the Wampanoag—the first tribe the Pilgrims encountered—at a home site based on how the group would have lived in the 17th century. In addition to the two settlements, the museum includes a visitor center, the Craft Center, barns that contain rare and heritage animal breeds, and a grist mill that manufactures cornmeal. Plymouth Patuxet Museums is also responsible for the replica of the Mayflower, the ship that brought the Pilgrims to the New World.
plimoth-patuxet-museums.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
plimoth-patuxet-museums.benefits_text: Patrons can purchase tickets online at plimoth.org or in person, when they arrive at the Museum. If purchasing tickets at the location the library coupon must be presented to Plimoth Patuxet Museums guest services staff upon arrival at the Museum. Patrons may purchase a Heritage Pass, Combination Pass, or Plimoth Patuxet Only pass, for up to six individuals at the reduced rate of $18 per adult; $12 per child ages 5-17. Pass membership also includes access to online educational lectures offered over Zoom. Visit the member events page at www.plimoth.org /events for more details. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch library.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: Each pass admits 2 adults at free or reduced admission (depending upon property rules) GO Pass user benefits: Free or reduced admission to Trustees properties The opportunity to get outside and explore places in Massachusetts that may not have been easily accessible to them before GO passes are valid for admission only; not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, Inns, Campgrounds, special events, and programs, on any equipment rental, or in place of any other permits or passes. Each GO Pass has information to be filled in on the back of the pass. This is to be filled in by the pass-user at the time of use at a Trustees property (Institution, Trustees property, date of use, name, email) Each GO Pass is a one-time use pass and is valid for admission only. Not every Trustees property charges an admission fee. For information about the admission at each property, visit us online thetrustees.org This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch library.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: One pass allows free admission for up to 5 people. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

wenham-museum.description: Wenham Museum is a history museum celebrating the treasures of childhood and New England family life across four centuries, with an emphasis on the North Shore. The museum's collections feature a world-renowned doll collection and dollhouses, costumes and textiles, model trains, antique toys, historic photographs, and the interactive Family Discovery Gallery. Admission includes a tour of the historic Claflin-Gerrish-Richards House, built at the end of the 17th century. In addition, there are rotating special programs and exhibitions. A gift shop is on the premises.
wenham-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
wenham-museum.benefits_text: This discount pass is good for 4 general admissions at half price. Reservations do not need to be made in advance. This pass is for general admission only and special programs the museum offers are not included in the pass ( this included Kids' Countdown to New Year's Eve (on New Year's Eve Day), Fairy Festival (TBD), or other programs that have special pricing)* The Wenham Museum is not open Easter, Independence Day, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day or New Year's Day. We are not open on Mondays - except for MLK Day, Presidents' Day and Veterans' Day. During mid May - Labor Day, we are not open on Sundays in the Summer. For admission for this pass, simply show the confirmation email on your phone at the front desk of the museum or bring a printed out copy. This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Please check Zoo New England website for updated information before visiting. Pass admits 6 people to Franklin Park Zoo (Boston) or Stone Zoo (Stoneham). Discounted rate is $9 per adult; $6 per child. Free admission for children under 2. ZOOLIGHTS is not discounted with pass. This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Branch library.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
