north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: North Shore Children’s Museum, located in downtown Peabody, MA, resides in a former TD bank building. The unique space is composed of 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2-8. Watch your child become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and so much more! In addition, NSCM hosts dozens of free programs each month, including yoga, Mini Movers, and live music. This is an ADA-accessible space. The reserved ePass pass allows for 50% off admission for a family of five (adults/children); it can either be presented on a mobile device or as a print-out to receive the discounted admission rate in-person. Passes will be emailed to reserving patrons, without the need to come to the library to pick up the pass! Please contact the library to reserve this pass if you do not have an email address. Due to the high volume of visitors during this popular month and upcoming months, we strongly encourage patrons to secure their passes in advance to guarantee their spots. This will help us manage ticket availability and ensure that everyone can enjoy their museum experience without any inconvenience. To purchase tickets online, visit https://www.nschildrensmuseum.org/visit/buy-tickets/ , click on "Buy Tickets" and select your preferred admission. Online Reservations: Patrons can reserve their museum passes through our online reservation system. This is the most efficient way to secure a spot. Phone Reservations: Patrons can also call us to hold their spots or check ticket availability for play sessions before visiting the North Shore Children's Museum. Your library pass will only be honored on the date you reserved. If the museum reaches capacity, visitors will be let in on a one in, one out basis.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Each pass admits two visitors at the discounted rate of $12 per ticket. Upon reserving the pass, you will receive a promo code that may be used online to purchase tickets at a discounted price. You do not need to pick up anything from the library. Timed museum entry via online reservation is required. Visit my.pem.org/events or call the reservation line at (978) 542-1511 to purchase your tickets. It is recommended to reserve your time as soon as you receive the promo code. To purchase your tickets online via my.pem.org/events : Enter the promo code in the upper-right box labeled “Promo code” and click “Submit.” The page will refresh. Click on the box that says "General Admission" under the heading "Choose an option below." Then, select a date and time for your visit. You will see an option for “Library discount $12” which will allow you to purchase discounted tickets. Sign in or register for an account to complete your transaction.

salem-witch-museum.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
salem-witch-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
salem-witch-museum.benefits_text: Please note, this pass is not valid in the month of October. Pass allows for a discounted admission rate for six visitors . After reserving this pass, it must be picked up at the Topsfield Library Circulation Desk during our hours of operation on the day of your reservation (or the business day before, if the library is closed the day of your reservation). The physical pass includes a special discount code. Purchase tickets for the Salem Witch Museum online using the discount code to receive discount. Present the physical pass upon arrival to the museum. Without the pass, you will be charged the full admission fee. This pass must be returned to the library by 10am the day after use.

sandmagination.description: Sandmagination is home to a number of sandboxes at the North Shore Mall in Peabody, MA. Aimed at children 10 and under, the sandboxes provide a hands-on, play-based learning experience, with STEAM-inspired activities like construction and tool usage. Toys and space are sanitized “constantly,” and the box is filled with allergen-free sand. There are also air purifiers onsite. While your child is playing, you can visit the café for coffee and free Wi-FI. Sandmagination is ADA-compliant. Check the website for hours, FAQs, or to sign the online waiver.
sandmagination.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
sandmagination.benefits_text: This coupon provides 50% off of all play passes for up to six guests per day. Admission allows for up to 2 hours of playtime at Sandmagination. Tickets can be reserved online on the Sandmagination website (upon reservation, the discount code will be provided) or purchased in-person at the front desk.

trustees-of-the-reservations.description: The Trustees of the Reservations preserve and protect more than 100 special properties of exceptional scenic, historic, and ecological value—nearly 25,000 acres—all around Massachusetts. From Crane's Beach in Ipswich and World's End in Hingham to The Old Manse in Concord and Fruitlands Museum in Harvard, visitors to Trustees sites enjoy local history and nature in its various forms: beaches, meadows, forests, swamps and more.
trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: The pass is valid for one-time use at any of 128 properties that collect an admission fee. Pass holder will pay an admission fee equivalent to that of a Trustees Family level membership (under most circumstances, this is free). Pass is not valid for use at Trustees parking kiosks, stores, cafes, Inns, campgrounds, at special events and programs, or in place of any permits or passes. View all Trustees properties here . After the pass is reserved, it must be picked up at the Topsfield Town Library Circulation Desk during our hours of operation. The pass does not need to be returned to the library. Due to high demand, advance admission passes to the most popular properties are either required or strongly encouraged . When reserving your pass, you will receive a promo code that you can use to receive the discounted rate.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 9 people to the USS Constitution Museum for free. Once reserved, simply present your library pass (either on your mobile device, or printed) at the admission window at the USS Constitution Museum for entry. The USS Constitution Museum, located in the Charlestown Navy Yard, serves as the memory and educational voice of USS Constitution by collecting, preserving, and interpreting the stories of “Old Ironsides” and the people associated with her. The museum offers a variety of engaging activities, exhibits, and programs, including our interactive exhibit, All Hands on Deck: A Sailor’s Life in 1812, where visitors can furl a sail, sleep in a hammock, and scrub the deck as they join Constitution’s crew in 1812! For more information about the museum’s galleries, programs, and resources, visit www.usscm.org . Passes will be emailed to reserving patrons, without the need to come to the library to pick up the pass! Please contact the library to reserve this pass if you do not have an email address.

wenham-museum.description: Wenham Museum is a history museum celebrating the treasures of childhood and New England family life across four centuries, with an emphasis on the North Shore. The museum's collections feature a world-renowned doll collection and dollhouses, costumes and textiles, model trains, antique toys, historic photographs, and the interactive Family Discovery Gallery. Admission includes a tour of the historic Claflin-Gerrish-Richards House, built at the end of the 17th century. In addition, there are rotating special programs and exhibitions. A gift shop is on the premises.
wenham-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
wenham-museum.benefits_text: This pass allows half-price admission for up to 4 people . The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the museum. Please note that advanced reservations are not required, but still available if interested by visiting the Wenham Museum website .

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: This coupon admits a maximum of 6 people at $9 per adult and $6 per child to the two Zoo New England destinations: the Stone Zoo in Stoneham, or the Franklin Park Zoo in Boston. After the pass is reserved, it must be picked up at the Topsfield Town Library Circulation Desk during our hours of operation . It is a paper coupon and does not need to be returned to the library. Please note: these passes can only be used for daytime admission. They cannot be used for Stone Zoo ZooLights or Franklin Park Zoo Boston Lights. To use the library discount, you must present the physical library ticket at the Zoo's admission booth. Reservations are not required for this discount.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits up to four people for half price. In July & August the museum is open Monday-Sunday From September - through June the museum is open Wednesday - Monday. During school vacations the museum is open on Tuesdays.

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: Passes admit 4 people for 50% off regular museum admission rates. Please check their website for the individual museums days and hours that they are open.

the-house-of-seven-gables.description: The House of the Seven Gables, one of the oldest surviving timber-framed mansion houses in continental North America, was the setting of Nathaniel Hawthorne's famous 1851 novel of the same name. The historic site which includes the house (a National Historic Landmark District) includes a guided tour of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion (the House of the Seven Gables) and visits to the Nathaniel Hawthorne Birthplace, the Colonial Revival seaside gardens, and the Counting House, with a maritime discovery zone called “Kids' Cove at The Gables.” (An audio tour of the gardens, grounds, and waterfront is available through the UniGuide App.) Lectures and seasonal living history labs are scheduled throughout the year, and a changing annual exhibition examines various aspects of the institution's 350 years of history. There is a gift shop on the premises. The website has virtual field trips and interactive exhibitions that can provide helpful background information before a visit.
the-house-of-seven-gables.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-house-of-seven-gables.benefits_text: Pass gives patrons half price admission for up to four people.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: Pass admits up to 2 visitors at the discounted price of $10 per admission. Admission is free to anyone 18 and under. The ICA is free to all on Thursday nights from 5:00-9:00pm. Please note that Museum hours and admission are subject to change. Current information can be found on their website: https://www.icaboston.org/visit Advance reservation strongly recommended. Cannot be used for performances or special events. Does not guarantee admittance to special exhibitions with timed entry.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 4 people for $5 a person. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted free. When reserving Gardner Museum timed entry tickets online, you will submit the promo code, select the correct date and your chosen timed entry, and continue checking out (the discount will be applied at checkout). Tickets must be reserved at least 48 hours prior to visiting—this library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum. If you have any issues reserving your tickets you can call the Gardner Museum's Box Office at 617-278-5156. Patrons must present both this library pass and Museum timed tickets for admission into the Museum. For more information, please visit: isgm.org/welcome .

john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.benefits_text: Passes are no longer required—the presentation of a valid library card grants the cardholder 50% off of one admission ticket—but please place a reservation for the desired date of your visit. Visitors aged 12 and under receive free admission. Please visit JFK Museum's website for more details and to reserve tickets in advance.

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: The one-day pass is valid for up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. At some sanctuaries there is a $2 admission fee per visitor over the age of 2.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: Pass provides free parking for 1 car

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 adults at $10 a person (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7-17 at $10 each (general and special exhibition). There is no discount for children 7-17 for general admisson, currently $10. Children under 6 are admitted free. Please note, as of January 20, 2026, there is a ticket price increase. Pass for two adult tickets will be ($15 each) and four youth tickets ($10 each). Promo codes are redeemable online, over the phone, or at any MFA ticket desk. Passes are not applicable toward discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses, or any other public programs. All prices are subject to change. Discount admission may not be combined with other offers. When planning your visit, please check www.mfa.org/visit for current Museum hours and early closings. The Museum is closed on New Year’s Day, Patriots’ Day, Fourth of July, Thanksgiving, and Christmas.

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: 1. This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under the age of 3 are also free, and do not count towards the 4 visitor limit. 2. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. 3. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. 4. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00am - 5:00pm daily). 5. If you are not able to show your Library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: · This coupon allows 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium for up to 4 people. · This is not a ticket. · Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission for up to four people. · From May 26-September 4, 2026 discounted passes are only available Monday-Friday. · Please visit https://www.neaq.org/visit/tickets/offers-discounts/ for details. · Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. · Valid only for Aquarium admission. · This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. · Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. · Void if altered or copied. No cash value. · Hours of operation are subject to change.

north-shore-childrens-museum.description: Located in downtown Peabody, MA, the North Shore Children's Museum offers 15 hands-on exhibits geared towards explorative and imaginative play for children ages 2 to 8. A child may become a superhero in the Comic Room, a builder in the Gross Motor Room, an artist in the Makerspace, and more. All exhibits are in ADA-accessible spaces.
north-shore-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
north-shore-childrens-museum.benefits_text: 50% off admission for a family of up to 5 adults/children. Tickets can be purchased in advance by selecting the Library Museum Pass option on the museum website. An ePass can either be presented on a mobile device or as a print-out to receive the discounted admission rate in-person. If the library pass is not presented at the museum, you will be charged the difference for full price tickets. Valid only on the date listed on the pass, subject to museum capacity. Due to a high volume of visitors, it is best to reserve museum tickets online or call the museum to hold tickets, or to confirm availability the day of before visiting the North Shore Children's Museum.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
peabody-essex-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
peabody-essex-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 people for $12 a person

plimoth-patuxet-museums.description: Plimoth Patuxet Museums offers a living history that tells the story of the English colonists who became known as “Pilgrims” and the Native Americans with whom they interacted. A big draw is the re-creation of the original settlement, populated with interpreters in period dress with whom one may interact that are based on actual historical figures. Native People from various nations in historically accurate dress provide information about the Wampanoag—the first tribe the Pilgrims encountered—at a home site based on how the group would have lived in the 17th century. In addition to the two settlements, the museum includes a visitor center, the Craft Center, barns that contain rare and heritage animal breeds, and a grist mill that manufactures cornmeal. Plymouth Patuxet Museums is also responsible for the replica of the Mayflower, the ship that brought the Pilgrims to the New World.
plimoth-patuxet-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
plimoth-patuxet-museums.benefits_text: This library pass must be presented to Plimoth Patuxet Museums guest services staff upon arrival at the Museum for Library patrons to purchase a Heritage Pass, Combination Pass, or Plimoth Patuxet Only pass for up to six individuals at the reduced rate of $18 per adult; $12 per child ages 5-17. This coupon is for one time use only by the patron. Visit plimoth.org/plan-your-visit for up to date visitor information including new safety protocols, or call 508-746-1622 for hours, directions or other information.

salem-witch-museum.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
salem-witch-museum.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
salem-witch-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 6 people at $14.00 for adults and $11.75 for children. You must bring the orange pass to the museum to receive the discounted price. Anyone arriving without the orange pass will need to pay full rate on arrival to the museum. Library passes are not accepted in the month of October.

trustees-of-the-reservations.description: The Trustees of the Reservations preserve and protect more than 100 special properties of exceptional scenic, historic, and ecological value—nearly 25,000 acres—all around Massachusetts. From Crane's Beach in Ipswich and World's End in Hingham to The Old Manse in Concord and Fruitlands Museum in Harvard, visitors to Trustees sites enjoy local history and nature in its various forms: beaches, meadows, forests, swamps and more.
trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: Pass admits 2 adults and children under 18. Prices vary by location.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: Pass admits 5 people for free, one pass per party.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: Pass admits 6 people at $9 per adult and $6 per child. Children under 2 are free.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: The pass admits up to 4 people at 1/2 off the general admission. Free admission for children under 1. *This is an e-pass which will be emailed to you and must be used on the selected date.*

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: A discount code will be emailed to you. This pass allows 50% off the ticket price for up to 4 people and does not have to be used on the date selected in the Library's pass system.

charles-river-museum-of-industry-and-inn.museum_name: Charles River Museum of Industry & Innovation
charles-river-museum-of-industry-and-inn.description: The Charles River Museum building, formerly known as the Francis Cabot Lowell Mill, dates back to 1813, when the Boston Manufacturing Company was founded. Visitors may trace the history of this development through the exhibit “The Mill at the Falls: the Evolution of the First Modern Factory.” This and other exhibits present artifacts from the industrial era, such as machines from the Waltham Watch Company, surgical products of the Rudolph Beaver Company, and several early motorized vehicles. The museum also displays contemporary technology and even prototypes, such as the Low Temperature, Low Pressure Plasma Medical Sterilizer by Dr. Adir Jacob. For children, visit the youth-led maker space, the Collaboratory, with 3D printers, Laser cutters, and more onsite. There is no visitor parking at the museum, but there are accessible entrances for wheelchair users and accessible restrooms. There is a gift shop on site.
charles-river-museum-of-industry-and-inn.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
charles-river-museum-of-industry-and-inn.benefits_text: Reduced admission to the museum for up to five guests per visit. ($5 for adults and free for children under 16)

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: This pass admits 3 visitors at 1/2 price admission per person. Any additional visitors must pay the regular admission price. *This is an e-pass which will be emailed to you and must be used on the selected date.* The Discovery Museum offers free admission for all on the first Friday of each month, October to June from 4:30 to 8:00pm.

gore-place.description: Gore Place is a historic 19th century country house located in Waltham, MA. Indoor and outdoor tours of the 50-acre estate—which boasts a magnificent Mansion, an authentic 1793 Carriage House, a working farm, and beautiful grounds—are available; check the website for the schedule. Special events include an annual sheepshearing festival and a summer concert series. There is a museum shop on the premises.
gore-place.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
gore-place.benefits_text: This pass is good for: Daytime Mansion Tour admission for two adults at $5 per person, and two children free One Take-a-Walk bag rental at $2/hr Story Time for two children at $5 per child, and an accompanying adult free For available dates and times, please visit goreplace.org/whats-on

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 visitors at 50% off regular museum admission rates. Discount may not be combined with other offers. *This is a physical, coupon pass which must be picked up at the Ground Floor Circulation Desk. This pass has an expiration date and does NOT need to be used on the date you've selected in our calendar.*

historic-new-england.address: 141 Cambridge Street, Administrative Offices: Otis House, Boston, MA 02114
historic-new-england.description: Historic New England is a historic preservation organization which owns and operates historic house museums and properties open to the public throughout most of New England. The majority of the 36 properties are in Massachusetts, but locations exist in all NE states but Vermont. These include Castle Tucker (Wiscasset, ME), the Jackson House (Portsmouth, NH), the Spencer-Pierce-Little Farm, which (Newbury, MA), Roseland Cottage (Woodstock, CT), the Otis House (Boston, MA), the Lyman Estate (Waltham, MA), Gropius House (Lincoln, MA), and the Arnold House (Lincoln, RI). Styles represented include Federal, Colonial Revival, Victorian, Queen Anne, Arts & Crafts, and Modern, among many others. The website provides much detail, including directions, contact information, and history of each property.
historic-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
historic-new-england.benefits_text: Free admission for up to 2 adults and their children or grandchildren (under 18) to one Historic New England site. *This is a physical, coupon pass which must be picked up at the Ground Floor Circulation Desk. This pass has an expiration date and does NOT need to be used on the date you've selected in our calendar.*

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: Admits up to 4 people at $5.00 each. Children under 18 are free. This pass is flexible and does not need to be used on the date it is reserved

mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.description: Mass Audubon maintains about 100 wildlife sanctuaries in Massachusetts, more than half of which may be visited for walks on trails and visits to nature centers and museums. Among these is Drumlin Farm in Lincoln, a working farm with animals and crops, plus exhibits that include native wildlife, such as owls, foxes and deer. A shop on the premises sells items related to nature, such as bird feeders and binoculars.
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
mass-audubon-drumlin-farm.benefits_text: This one-day Library Partner Admission Pass admits up to 4 people to a Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. With this pass, admission is discounted to $2 per visitor over the age of 2. Please visit https://www.massaudubon.org/get-outdoors/wildlife-sanctuaries/trails for the latest information, including safety guidelines. Please Note: Drumlin Farms is only open on Mondays when it is a holiday. Otherwise you can visit Tuesday-Sunday.

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: This pass allows one vehicle to park for free at any MA State Parks. *This is a paper parking pass which must be picked up at the Ground Floor Circulation Desk and used on the date selected. The pass can be picked up on the day of or day before use and must be returned by the day following use.*

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: For visit dates beginning January 20, 2026, each promo code may be redeemed for up to 2 adult tickets for $15 each and 4 youth tickets for $10 each. Promo codes are redeemable online, over the phone, or at any MFA ticket desk. Passes are not applicable toward discounts on shopping, parking, dining, films, concerts, lectures, courses, or any other public programs. All prices are subject to change. Discount admission may not be combined with other offers. When planning your visit, please check www.mfa.org/visit for current Museum hours and early closings. The Museum is closed on New Year’s Day, Patriots’ Day, Independence Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day." *This is an e-pass which will be emailed to you and must be used on the selected date.*

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.

museum-of-science.benefits_text: This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. *This is an e-pass which will be emailed to you and must be used on the selected date.* Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Halls tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Halls passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily).

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.

This pass has an expiration date and does NOT need to be used on the date you've selected in our calendar.*

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.

This pass has an expiration date and does NOT need to be used on the date you've selected in our calendar.*

armenian-museum-of-america.description: With roots going back to the early 1970s, The Armenian Museum of America has occupied its Watertown location since 1988. The largest collection of Armenian cultural artifacts outside of Armenia, the 25,000 objects include five of the first printed bibles in Armenian from 1666, ancient Urartian artifacts, medieval manuscripts, K�tahya ceramics, textiles, liturgical objects, and contemporary art. With such a large collection, the museum estimates that only around 5% of its holding are on view at a given time, through rotating and permanent exhibits, including explorations of modern day Armenian-American culture, artworks by contemporary Armenian and Armenian-American artists, and an exhibit focusing on the Armenian Genocide. All galleries are wheelchair accessible, and there are HEPA Air Purifiers throughout. There is a gift shop featuring work by Armenian vendors.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.

garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.

More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.

peabody-essex-museum.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.

boston-harbor-island-ferry.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
boston-harbor-island-ferry.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-harbor-island-ferry.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Discount code is e-mailed to the patron. The discount code provides access to purchase tickets at 50% off the regular price for the following: Spectacle Island May 16 - June 15: Thursdays through Sundays June 16 - Sep 1: every day Sep 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays Sep 26 - Oct 13: Fridays through Sundays Georges Island June 16 - Sep 1: every day Spectacle Island June 16 - Sep 1: every day Sep 4 - Oct 13: Thursdays through Sundays Ferries to Georges and Spectacle Islands are boarded at Long Wharf North in Downtown Boston. Ferries to Peddocks Island are boarded from Hingham.

davis-farmland.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
davis-farmland.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
davis-farmland.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon . Patrons must pick up their pass at the Library up to one week before their visit. It does not need to be returned to the library. Each pass admits up to 6 people to either farm park with a savings of $10 per person off the purchased-at-the-gate general admission rate. Children under 2 are always free. Davis Farmland Discovery Farm is open mid-April - October. Davis Mega Maze is open mid-September - October. Pass is not valid with other discounts, groups or special events. For the most up-to-date information on programs, hours, or closing due to weather, please visit the David Farmland website , or call (978) 422-MOOO (6666). Adults must be accompanied by a child 12-years of age and under.

the-discovery-museums.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
the-discovery-museums.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-discovery-museums.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the patron. Only Valid for the Reserved Date of Visit. Each coupon allows for admission of three people to the Museum at half price off regular admission. Children under age one are free. A library coupon is NOT a museum entry reservation. Advanced museum entry reservation is not available for Library Pass users. Present your Library Pass confirmation page to Admissions staff when checking in and the discount will be applied to the cost of your visit. Please consult the Plan Your Visit page on the Museum website for the most up-to-date policies, hours, and information.

ecotarium.description: The EcoTarium is a unique indoor-outdoor museum in Worcester, MA. Set in an urban oasis, the museum offers a chance to walk through the treetops, take a multimedia journey through the galaxy at a digital planetarium, meet wildlife, stroll nature trails, and get hands-on with family-friendly exhibits.
ecotarium.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
ecotarium.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each coupon admits up to 2 adults and 2 children in one vehicle at 50% off admission. Children under 2 are always free. When using the Library Pass discount, tickets cannot be reserved online - tickets must be purchased in person. These coupons do not cover the cost for tickets to the Alden Planetarium, the Explorer Express Train, or other special ticketed events. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website.

garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
garden-in-the-woods-native-plant-trust.benefits_text: This is a SET OF 2 laminated passes, each pass offers discount to ONE person: Adults (age 18 and over) receive 50% the regular admission price. Children (age 6 to 17) are admitted for $1. Children (age 5 and younger) are admitted for free. Patrons may pick up their passes the day before or the day of their visit. The passes must be returned to the Wayland Library by the day after your visit . Tickets are available at the Visitor Center when you arrive. Please check their website for weather closings, special events, parking updates, etc. before visiting.

gore-place.description: Gore Place is a historic 19th century country house located in Waltham, MA. Indoor and outdoor tours of the 50-acre estate—which boasts a magnificent Mansion, an authentic 1793 Carriage House, a working farm, and beautiful grounds—are available; check the website for the schedule. Special events include an annual sheepshearing festival and a summer concert series. There is a museum shop on the premises.
gore-place.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
gore-place.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Coupon is emailed to the patron. Only Valid for the Reserved Date of Visit. This coupon is good for: - Daytime Mansion Tour admission for two adults at $5 per person, and two children free (regularly $16 per adult and $11 for children ages 6-16). Mansion tours are offered Monday, Wednesday, Friday, and Saturdays. -One Take-a-Walk bag rental at $2/hr (regularly $5). Take-a-Walk bags for youth ages 4 and up contain outdoor seasonal activities for up to two children and are available Monday-Saturday. -Story Time for two children at $5 per child, and an accompanying adult free. Please note: The Mansion Tour coupon is good for daytime Mansion tours only and may not be used during evening or special themed tours or during special events such as the Sheep Shearing Festival at Gore Place. Days and times are subject to change, so it is best for visitors to consult the museum website when planning your visit. Event and holiday closings as well as school vacation hours are all listed on goreplace.org/whats-on .

harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each coupon will allow four patrons into the museums at a 50% off rate regardless of age / admission level. Please be prepared to show the physical library pass and proof of Massachusetts residency. Only one member of the party must be a MA resident. The pass allows admission to both the Harvard Museum of Natural History and Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, as well as two free museums, the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East (formerly the Harvard Semitic Museum) and the Harvard Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments. Patrons will enter through the Natural History or Peabody Museum with their hard copy pass. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit hmsc.harvard.edu .

institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
institute-of-contemporary-art-boston.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This library pass entitles you to up to two tickets for Gallery Admission at the discounted Library Pass price of $10 per ticket. Library Passes are not valid for performances, events, or films. This library pass is not a museum admission ticket. After reserving your library pass, follow the emailed instructions on how to reserve your free Gallery Admission tickets.

isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
isabella-stewart-gardner-museum.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Promo code and confirmation page are e-mailed to the patron. Each pass provides up to four (4) people with $5 admission per person. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted for free. You must print your library pass confirmation page or display it on your phone. The Museum requires also reserving timed-entry tickets in advance of your visit. PATRONS MUST PRESENT BOTH THE LIBRARY PASS AND THEIR RESERVED TIMED MUSEUM ADMISSION TICKETS FOR ENTRY INTO THE MUSEUM. A library pass is not an admission ticket and does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.

john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
john-f-kennedy-library-and-museum.benefits_text: No pass needed, just your library card! Present your physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive a 50% discount on one admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and EACH VISITOR must present a Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. You must have your physical or digital library card in hand at the museum; the museum cannot access the library system to look up your account. A digital version of your library card can be accessed on your mobile device via the Minuteman Library Network app. You will need to know your account username or library card number, and your library account password or PIN. Your password can be reset at https://wayland.minlib.net/MyAccount/Home . Visitors aged 12 and under always receive free admission, with or without a library card. Please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Services Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website, jfklibrary.org .

mass-audubon.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
mass-audubon.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
mass-audubon.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the Patron; Print out the pass or display it on your phone at the Museum entrance. Each pass admits up to 4 people at $2 per person to any Massachusetts Audubon Wildlife Sanctuary, including Drumlin Farm in Lincoln, MA. (Children under age 2 are always free.) Holiday calendar, days open and hours of operation vary by property - please check the Mass Audubon website, massaudubon.org , for more details. Drumlin Farm Hours: Tuesday–Sunday, 9am-5pm • Closed Mondays (except holidays) • Closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day. For the most up-to-date information on the Drumlin Farm programs and hours, please visit their website .

massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-parks-department-of-.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Pass allows free daytime parking for one carload of visitors to all Massachusetts State Parks that charge a parking fee. (Except Nahant and Nantasket). See the Massachusetts Department of Conservation & Recreation website for the list of parks that charge a fee and the most up-to-date information on park programs and hours.

massachusetts-state-police-museum-and-le.museum_name: Massachusetts State Police Museum & Learning Center
massachusetts-state-police-museum-and-le.description: The Massachusetts State Police Museum and Learning Center is located on the site of an old state police barracks built in 1931 in in Grafton, MA. Visitors may explore the agency's past, as well as learn about current law enforcement methods and public safety efforts. Exhibits include a range of State Police-related items and historic memorabilia.
massachusetts-state-police-museum-and-le.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
massachusetts-state-police-museum-and-le.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Pass allows free admission for 2 people age 16+. Kids age 15 & under are always free. The Museum is open Tuesdays and Saturdays from 11am-4pm. Please see the Museum's website ( mspmlc.org ) for the most up-to-date information.

museum-of-fine-arts.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
museum-of-fine-arts.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-fine-arts.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Promo Code is e-mailed to the patron. Each Promo Code use allows 2 people at $15 per adult, $10 per child age 7-17. Children 6 and under are admitted for free. Note: The date of your museum visit must match the date on the library pass. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website .

museum-of-science.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
museum-of-science.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
museum-of-science.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This pass offers up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are always admitted for free. A library pass is not a museum admission ticket. See emailed instructions for how to purchase your tickets at the discounted rate. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit the museum's website.

new-england-aquarium.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
new-england-aquarium.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
new-england-aquarium.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the patron. This coupon allows 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium for up to 4 people. This Library pass is not an Aquarium entry ticket. Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission. Advanced reservations are not available for library patrons with coupons. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. Review the Aquarium’s safety guidelines at neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. -Valid only for Aquarium admission. -This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. -Applies only to regularly priced Adult and Child tickets -Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. -Void if altered. No cash value. -Hours of operation are subject to change.

new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
new-england-botanic-garden-at-tower-hill.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Each pass offers 2 people half price general admission. Discount not valid for special events such as Night Lights. The physical pass from the library must be shown at check-in. The Library Discount Pass is not an entry ticket to the Garden. For the most up-to-date information on New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill's programs and hours, please visit their website.

the-trustees-of-the-reservations.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
the-trustees-of-the-reservations.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each pass provides free or reduced admission for 2 adults and any number of children (under the age of 18) at 116 preserved lands and estates across Massachusetts, including Fruitlands and Old Manse. Discounts vary between properties. Please note, each pass is valid only toward general admission fees and not special events. The Library coupon is not an admission ticket. Follow emailed instructions to purchase tickets at the Library Discount rate. For more information, and for preserve locations, please visit the organization's website at www.thetrustees.org or call 978-921-1944. NOTE: deCordova is CLOSED Mondays & Tuesdays.

the-uss-constitution-museum.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
the-uss-constitution-museum.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
the-uss-constitution-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
the-uss-constitution-museum.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This pass admits 5 people for free. You can either present a printed Library pass or display it from your phone or other device at the Museum. Valid only for the date listed on the pass. Limit one pass per party. Please note that the ship and museum have different hours. Museum Hours: Open Daily; Winter Hours: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Summer Hours: 9:00AM - 6:00PM. The Museum closes for Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day, and closes early on Christmas Eve.

zoo-new-england.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
zoo-new-england.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
zoo-new-england.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each pass admits up to 6 persons at $9 per adult and $6 per child (ages 2-12). Children under 2 are admitted for free. The library discount coupon is not a Zoo admission ticket. Your coupon can be redeemed in person, and tickets can be purchased at the gate. Online reservations are not needed. Library discount passes may be used for general daytime admission only. They cannot be used for evening events such as Boston Lights. For the most up-to-date information on zoo programs and hours, please visit their website.

boston-childrens-museum.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
boston-childrens-museum.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
boston-childrens-museum.benefits_text: This pass is good for 50% off admission for up to 4 people. Boston Children's Museum will be closed September 8, 2025 -September 16, 2025 for regularly scheduled Annual Maintenance. try {var w = document.getElementsByClassName("warn"); for( i=0;i
